I had a deeply disappointing experience at this property during an already difficult time, as my family and I were in New Jersey to bury my grandmother
Upon check-in, the room condition did not match the hotel’s description as “recently renovated
” The room appeared worn, the pillows had an unpleasant odor, and the shower had a damp, musty smell
When I contacted the front desk shortly after check-in to request a room change or cancellation, the associate was dismissive and unhelpful
That same night, my family who were staying in a second room, were displaced by a fire alarm that was not resolved until early morning
They were unable to safely remain in the hotel and had to sleep in their car overnight
The following day, there was also no hot water available at the property
Between the room conditions, safety disruption, lack of hot water, and the way concerns were handled by staff, this stay fell far below basic hospitality standards, especially for a Hilton property
As a premium Hilton Honors member, this experience was unacceptable and extremely disappointing
The hotel has nice facilities, clean rooms, and good housekeeping (I stay here about once a month)

The hotel has a captive clientele of professional pilots (I am one)

The problem is the regular evacuation alarms (which destroys your nights rest)
In the last three years I’ve experienced 18 alarms at this hotel
It’s common enough that the regulars at the bar will occasionally comment “I hope we don’t have another fire alarm tonight”
It’s a little hard to take the alarms seriously anymore (but the alarm is indeed piercingly loud and usually lasts 1-3 hours)
If they are false, it’s really annoying, if they are real, you have to wonder what’s wrong with the facility that generates alarms that regularly
Outside in the cold the only answers you get are the frustrating and condescending “we don’t know” and “it’s for your own safety”

Additionally, the lighting near the elevators, especially on the upper floors, was quite dim and felt unsafe — it could really use improvement
The in-hotel restaurant was a bit confusing to navigate
The staff seemed inconsistent in enforcing policies about where you could sit or whether outside food was allowed in certain areas
Better communication and flexibility would go a long way
Overall, the hotel has potential, but these issues — lighting, climate control, and unclear policies — were noticeable during my visit
It may work better for smaller events or guests simply passing through, but for large-scale gatherings, some upgrades are definitely needed

I normally do not leave reviews, but I felt it was important to share this experience because of Kim at the front desk
Overall, my stay was good
The hotel itself was comfortable and my interactions with staff after check-in were genuinely pleasant and professional
Unfortunately, my very first interaction when I arrived left a lasting negative impression
When I checked in on January 30, I was assisted by a front desk employee whose name tag read Kim
From the start, the interaction felt rushed and dismissive
When I did not immediately understand certain things, she made hand gestures at me to move faster rather than taking a moment to explain
She also didn’t bother to look at me while dismissively gesturing, which made it feel even worse
The overall tone felt impatient and unwelcoming, and I genuinely felt that she did not care how I felt in that moment whatsoever

Maybe I could have been more prepared coming to the front desk with what she may have needed? Or said something differently? I’m not sure if she thought she could act that at towards me just because I’m younger? I’m just not sure
I was a bit tired after walking through the freezing cold for awhile to get there, but I don’t think any of my actions warranted her response and treatment of me
I want to be clear that this is not about policy or procedure
It was about how I was treated
As a guest, regardless of age, I expect basic courtesy and respect
That first interaction was my introduction to the hotel, and it set a tone that lingered longer than it should have
What stood out even more was how different this experience was compared to the rest of your staff, who were consistently kind, helpful, and professional throughout my stay
Because of that contrast, this interaction felt especially disappointing
I am sharing this feedback with the hope that it helps ensure all guests feel welcomed and respected from the moment they arrive
The staff

We recently stayed at the Hilton Garden Inn on W
35th St
in Manhattan, and we couldn't be happier with our choice! The location is simply unbeatable
Just a short walk from Penn Station, we found it incredibly easy to navigate the city with public transportation right at our fingertips
The area is bustling with life, filled with a fantastic array of restaurants, food carts, and shopping options with Macys a stone throw away
Being so close to Madison Square Garden and the iconic Empire State Building was a real treat
In fact, we were lucky enough to have a room with a stunning view of the Empire State Building – it made for some unforgettable moments!
As for the rooms, they are standard for a Hilton Garden Inn, which means they are on the smaller side (as expected in NYC), but they were impeccably clean and well-maintained
The amenities were average, but that didn’t detract from our experience
One thing to note is that while the hotel doesn't have a full-fledged restaurant, the breakfast option they offered was quite good, albeit a bit different from the typical Hilton Garden Inn spread
There’s also a bar that operates during the week, though it’s closed on weekends, which was a slight letdown for us
The outdoor seating area looked cozy and inviting, perfect for a relaxing moment between adventures
We also appreciated the gym in the basement, which allowed us to keep up with our fitness routines
Plus, the pantry near the front desk was stocked with all sorts of goodies for snacks and essentials
The staff were incredibly helpful and attentive, making our stay all the more enjoyable
Given how busy the hotel was, we were impressed by how well they managed everything
They even arranged a car service to pick us up for our flight
Overall, we truly enjoyed our stay at the Hilton Garden Inn on W
35th St
and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable and conveniently located spot in the heart of Manhattan!

However, the front desk staff on duty that evening denied our request and referred us to the storage staff, who also declined
We were told to leave our bike outside on the street, so we opted to pay for a private parking space nearby to keep it safe

The next morning, when we checked out and left our bags in the storage room, we asked again—this time with different staff—and were told that we could have stored our bike in the storage room after all

Unexpected gem
A dear friend passed, and I needed a quick, quiet, easy to access place in the city to begin from

I letti non sono nemmeno matrimoniali standard, ma da una piazza e mezza, pur trattandosi di una camera venduta come “quadrupla”
Unica nota positiva: il personale è stato sempre cortese e disponibile

While the staff wasn't explicitly rude, the front desk team could significantly improve their helpfulness and welcoming attitude
There were several issues that detracted from the overall experience:
Room phone functionality: The in-room phone numbers for housekeeping and other services didn't work
If these services aren't available via phone, it would be better to remove the phone or clearly communicate alternative contact methods
Water accessibility: It was inconvenient to have to go to the reception and then be directed to the workout area on a different floor just to get drinking water
The hotel should either provide water in rooms or clearly communicate where guests can easily access it
Digital key issues: When the Hilton app's digital key feature didn't work, the office staff's response to my request for help was condescending and unhelpful
A more patient and solution-oriented approach would have been appreciated
Overall service attitude: The staff seemed indifferent to guests' needs and concerns, which significantly impacted the stay quality
Due to these issues, particularly the poor service experience, I won't be returning to this location
As a paying customer, I expected a higher standard of service and accommodation

The hotel has 10 rooms per floor wrapped around 3 elevators - so there might be elevator noise (although I didn't hear it on a high floor)  They are associated with a parking garage where you drop the car off and you can charge the parking to your room (for normal size cars only - not oversized) which is a great convenience for business expense purposes
The only downside is that the breakfast area is a-la-carte and set up like a Starbucks with grab-and-go items
You only get $10 as a Diamond credit and they charge $4 for a 12-oz cup of coffee (with 1 refill)
I do not believe that there was coffee available in the lobby
As strange as it sounds, I have become accustomed to getting hot coffee as I enter or leave hotels and this is a rather big disadvantage for me
While there is a Keurig in the room, it just isn't the same
Given this 1 downside, I would consider other Hilton properties - since there are ones nearby that have similar rooms and slightly better amenities (like coffee)
NOTE: I post reviews only so I can remember places that I have stayed for business travel
What I consider important is solely my discretion and I am not trying to influence anyone's decisions or get an action from the hotel

Great location, but with typical NYC room size limitations
The Hilton Garden Inn on West 35th Street boasts a superb location
It's right across from Macy's Herald Square and near multiple subway lines, making everything in NYC easily accessible
The hotel itself has a well-stocked shop in the reception area, which was a lifesaver for grabbing midnight snacks
The staff was friendly and helpful, providing everything I needed during my stay
However, it's important to be aware that this is a mid-range hotel with typical New York City room limitations
The rooms are on the smaller side, which is common for the city
Additionally, while there is a microwave, there weren't any microwave-safe dishes available to heat up food
The HVAC unit also lacked a fan-only option, which made it chilly at times since I couldn't adjust it to just circulate air
Overall, this hotel is a solid choice for budget-minded travelers who prioritize location and convenience over spacious accommodations and high-end amenities

Unser Aufenthalt im Hilton Garden Inn New York/West 35th Street war insgesamt gemischt
Während es einige Aspekte gab, die uns enttäuschten, gab es auch positive Seiten
||Der Check-in verlief schnell und unpersönlich, und auch im weiteren Verlauf unseres Aufenthalts war das Personal überwiegend neutral und zeigte wenig Engagement
Besonders enttäuschend war eine Erfahrung, die wir nach einem heftigen Regenschauer machten
Ich war mit meiner Frau und unserem kleinen Kind unterwegs, und wir kamen komplett durchnässt ins Hotel zurück
Als wir in den Aufzug stiegen, wurden wir von einem Mitarbeiter nur skeptisch angesehen
Statt uns ein Handtuch anzubieten oder irgendwie behilflich zu sein, ließ man uns klatschnass im hochklimatisierten Hotel stehen
Diese fehlende Aufmerksamkeit und Fürsorge empfanden wir als sehr unangenehm, zumal wir mit einem kleinen Kind unterwegs waren
||Außerdem bin ich vor dem Hotel auf dem rutschigen Boden gestürzt, was aber meine Schuld war, da ich wegen des Regens gerannt bin
Obwohl das Hotelpersonal nicht direkt verantwortlich war, hätte ich mir zumindest eine nachträgliche Nachfrage nach meinem Wohlbefinden gewünscht
||Dennoch gab es auch positive Aspekte während unseres Aufenthalts
Die Lage des Hotels ist hervorragend, mitten im Herzen von Manhattan und nur wenige Schritte von vielen Sehenswürdigkeiten entfernt
Das Zimmer war sauber und gut ausgestattet, was für eine angenehme Nachtruhe sorgte
Besonders praktisch fanden wir die Annehmlichkeiten wie den kleinen Kühlschrank und die Mikrowelle im Zimmer, die uns mit uns sehr zugute kamen
Da nur wenige Zimmer pro Etage sind, ist es auf dem Flur sehr ruhig, auch wenn man gelegentlich den Aufzug hört
Für New Yorker Hotels ist es jedoch angenehm ruhig

||**Tipp für andere Hotelgäste:**||Um euren Aufenthalt im Hilton Garden Inn New York/West 35th Street angenehmer zu gestalten, empfehle ich, bei schlechtem Wetter einen zusätzlichen Regenschirm und ein Handtuch dabei zu haben, da das Personal hier nicht immer proaktiv Hilfe anbietet
Nutzt die zentrale Lage des Hotels, um die nahegelegenen Attraktionen zu Fuß zu erkunden, und macht euch die praktischen Annehmlichkeiten im Zimmer zunutze

On the third day I received a check-in text from the desk asking how things are and I repeated that we still had no wifi
No response
My daughter called support again and spent another half hour on the phone with no result
The next day I had a text conversation with the front desk

)  He then offered to show me on his phone that the wifi worked
I complained and asked what good that would do us if his  phone worked
He finally apologized and offered to comp the expensive $27+tax daily parking fee for our week
Upon arriving home I found we had been billed by the hotel even though we had prepaid through a third party
I called the hotel, who told me to email the manager and gave me her email address
After emailing on Monday I had heard nothing by Friday, so I called her and she said it was in process of being refunded
When it finally was the next week, it was for almost $40 less than what we had originally been billed
In looking at the invoice I see they had neglected to comp us for one day of parking
I replied by email explaining this and including screenshots from my text conversation where this was agreed upon
No answer
After two weeks I emailed again, including screenshots of the texts, invoices, etc
, and got no response
This is not an inexpensive hotel and to be treated in such a shoddy fashion is unacceptable to me

At 9:00 AM, I called the front desk to request fresh towels and specifically asked for them to be left outside my room
By 11:00 AM, they had still not arrived
Meanwhile, multiple staff members were gathered in a guest room down the hall, sitting and eating with a cart of towels nearby
I called again to repeat my request and, before leaving for lunch, placed a “Do Not Disturb” sign on my door
This was intentional, as there were prescription medications clearly visible in the room, and I did not want anyone entering while we were out
Despite this, staff entered the room anyway
When we returned, the towels had been placed inside the bathroom
The “Do Not Disturb” sign was visibly hanging on the door handle; there is no reasonable explanation for ignoring it
This is a serious violation of guest privacy and basic hotel standards
When I contacted the front desk, my concerns were dismissed and I was met with pushback instead of accountability
To make matters worse, I realized shortly after checkout (within 10 minutes) that I had left a bathing suit hanging in the shower
I called immediately and was later told it was never turned in to lost and found
Given that staff had already entered my room despite the “Do Not Disturb” sign, this is extremely concerning
At best, this reflects a complete lack of professionalism and protocol
At worst, it raises serious questions about the handling of guests’ personal belongings
I would strongly recommend management review their staff practices, particularly regarding guest privacy and lost item procedures

We usually don’t leave reviews, but we felt it was important to share our experience to help future guests better understand what they might encounter during their stay
First and foremost, the location was absolutely stunning
We stayed in a room on the 22nd floor, which offered breathtaking views of the river—a definite highlight of our trip
However, there were several issues that impacted our overall experience:
Upon arrival, the staff was unable to locate our reservation, which caused unnecessary stress after a long flight from California
Thankfully, they eventually found it, but the initial confusion was unsettling
The room itself was uncomfortably stuffy, and the air conditioning struggled to maintain a comfortable temperature throughout our stay, making it difficult to relax
Attempts to contact the front desk from our room were frustratingly unsuccessful
No one answered the phone, and messages sent through the app went unanswered
We had to go directly to the front desk to address any concerns
Unfortunately, no room service was provided during our stay
This left us with unmade beds, overflowing trash cans, wet towels on the floor, and empty body wash dispensers, which was disappointing
Even after speaking directly with housekeeping and the front desk, room service was still not provided
Instead, we were handed towels to carry up ourselves and given two small bars of soap as a substitute, which felt inadequate
We were only informed about the credits available to us on the night before checkout, leaving us scrambling to use them last-minute at the gift shop for souvenirs
Overall, the staff appeared indifferent and not particularly interested in assisting guests
We hope this feedback helps improve the experience for future visitors, as the location truly has so much potential

However, several issues with the room which made the stay less comfortable than expected
Our reservation was for two guests, and while the bathroom had two towels and washcloths, one towel had multiple stains
We called housekeeping for replacements, but after an hour with no update, we called again
The guest services rep said they would follow up, but another 35–40 minutes passed with no response
Eventually, I went down to the front desk, where they acknowledged the request had been shared, they provided towels from the back
Ironically, housekeeping arrived at the front desk to inquire on my request, as the towels were provided
After a long day and a late check‑in, we were simply hoping for a nice shower and rest
Additional concerns included a heater/air conditioner that didn’t work—the fan never turned on—and a toilet that ran continuously
The shower head’s water setting couldn’t be adjusted, and there were no hand towels provided for initial pre-room service
Hand towels were provided with the first housekeeping clean
Since we were only staying one more night, we chose not to call in the maintenance issues
Despite the inconveniences, the harbor view and the hotel’s location were genuinely enjoyable and made exploring the area easy
With some improvements to service responsiveness and room maintenance, this could be a much better experience

I recently had the pleasure of dining and staying at this Hilton, and I can say without exaggeration that the staff here turned a great visit into an unforgettable one
Three individuals, in particular, deserve the highest recognition for their hospitality
Emad (Manager)
From the moment we arrived at The Boat, Emad set the tone for the entire experience
He greeted us with a genuine, radiant smile and a level of warmth that is hard to find
What stood out most was his kindness toward others; seeing him take the time to play with a baby boy at the neighboring table showed his true character
He is full of life, engages in wonderful conversation, and ensures every guest feels seen and cared for
His "encouraging goodbye" was the perfect finishing touch to a stellar meal
Sian (Security)
As a young woman traveling, I cannot overstate how much I appreciated Sian’s presence
He went above and beyond his "job description" to ensure my safety and comfort
He personally verified that my Uber was the correct vehicle before I stepped in, held every door, and maintained a professional yet incredibly kind demeanor
His consistent "good mornings" and farewells made me feel secure and looked after
His kindness will truly never be forgotten
James (Server & Bartender)
James is a master of his craft
He was incredibly gracious and possessed deep knowledge of both the dinner and drink menus
His recommendations were spot on! Even more impressive was his attentiveness… even on days when I wasn't dining at the restaurant, James would make a point to greet me and my sister feel welcome
That level of recognition is what separates good service from world-class hospitality
This team doesn't just provide a service; they provide a community
If you are looking for a place where you are treated like family, look no further
Thank you, Emad, Sian, and James! We will be back

They gave us a drink voucher and luckily there is a restaurant in the hotel so we could have dinner while waiting for our room to be ready
After dinner our room was ready and we were upgraded to a larger room for the inconvenience
My husband also got some help bringing our bags upstairs from an employee named Kevin
Thank you so much! And before I forget, dinner was very good with warm and friendly seating from Ky and great service from Joe

||I will also add as friendly advice that Hilton would do well to provide clear/consistent guidance and standardize practices around how members can earn points on multiple rooms

I did exactly as instructed in the FAQ’s and requested that all charges be on one folio (one bill) though the hotel was confused and/or adamant that they could not do this and the room charges had to be on separate bills

One of the worst, certainly the most unique, hotel lodging experience of my life
We stayed at this hotel on 12/30/25

At approximately 4:30 AM we, along with everyone else staying in the hotel, were awoken to a blaring alarm/siren with an automated message indicating there had been an emergency on site and to remain on standby
30-40 minutes go by and the same alarm and message again are blared over the speaker system
After about an hour had passed from the initial alarm and no update had been provided, I called the front desk to see what was happening and was told there was no emergency
A drunk, belligerent guest had set off the alarm and that was all
So we were rattled awake in the early morning for an emergency, which at the time we thought could have been anything, and turned out there was no emergency at all
This was alarming and unnerving
We did not know whether we should evacuate or stay in our room because there was literally no further update or instruction from the hotel management or staff
At no point did the hotel update us, make an announcement that there was no emergency, apologize, or make any attempt to restitute the situation
If we didn’t call the front desk, we would have had no idea what was really happening
To say we were shocked is an understatement
Whoever the standing manager was at the time should be relieved of their duties to their negligence and gross mishandling of the situation
I plan on calling management or Hilton corporate offices after the holiday to lodge formal complaints
Regardless, we will NEVER stay at this hotel again

I am a Diamond Hilton member and have stayed at this hotel previously without issue
However, I am posting this review because the hotel refuses to acknowledge or rectify the horrible sanitary conditions of the room
On first glance, the hotel room was lovely with three windows on the corner facing the river
Though dated, that is not a problem
What is a problem is absolute filthy conditions of the bathroom, towels, and linens & the staff's inability to rectify
When I arrived, I noticed the bathroom floor was covered in bits of hair - I wiped up what I could and threw in the trash bin
The light switch had grime and brown dirt all over it - I wiped that down as well
The shower curtain was not snapped in properly as the plastic buttons were warped
When I went to shower, the shower head prayed in all directions so I decided to wipe it clean to allow water flow
What I cleaned left moldy bits, water scum, and debris coating the wash cloth
When I grabbed a towel to dry off it was stained orange
Then the second towel had hair strands woven into the fabric
The hand towel I used had weird brown / red stains
The final hand towel is what I used to dry off as it was the ONLY clean linen in the room
I stayed for two nights and no one came to clean the room despite never putting the 'do not disturb' sign out
When I notified the front desk about ALL the issues - the gentleman was horrified at the photos! He said he would have housekeeping come to clean my room at 8pm when we left for dinner
He provided us with fresh towels but as he handed them off, he noticed the wash cloths had hairs woven into the fabric - he promptly went to get fresh ones
We came back at 11pm and no one had come to clean the room
The next morning when we use the "fresh" towels, we found those to also have long strands of hair woven in the fabric
We used one towel for two people
Upon checkout, we told the front desk clerk the entire saga & expressed how truly disappointed we were in the sanitary & cleanliness of our stay
She apologized but offered no compensation or even Hilton points to rectify
Overall, the staff was kind and listened but that does not negate the overall sanitary conditions of the hotel much less their desire to rectify the issue when notified

The location was convenient for transferring to and from the airport, and being picked up by a colleague for work

The room was comfortable enough but the walls seemed paper-thin, with the conversations of other guests and noise from the outdoor A/C units clearly audible much of the time
The room was also in need of repairs and deeper cleaning, with some damaged fixtures, staining on the walls and curtain pulls, and visible dust and debris on the carpets and bath tub
Unfortunately, on my last morning, there was no hot water and then the water ran brown, so I was unable to have a shower

I expect this is the reason that I was not asked how my stay was during check-out, and when I did question the water issues there was no real apology given - only an excuse that the city had been doing some plumbing works

While I appreciate that the water issues may have not been caused through any fault of the hotel (although I’ve since seen at least one other review about no hot water from ~2 months’ before my stay, so perhaps there is an inherent issue with the hotel’s own water system), an apology and even a small goodwill gesture would have gone a long way to have shown that this hotel really cared for its guests

After being told someone would come to replace the machine, a housekeeping worker proceeded to visit our room three (yes, three, for one machine replacement) separate times, the first to check the issue and see if the machine was 'really' broken (it most definitely was), the second to come and bring in a 'new' machine (looked many years older than the one provided), and the third to come bring coffee and test the machine
For the rest of the stay, it was thankfully uneventful, but there was just an overall cold feel to this property
For the entirety of the stay, not a single staff member said hello to us, or greeted us, or opened property doors for us

I had an incredibly disappointing experience during my stay at this Hilton Club The Central at 5th New York property, which is particularly disheartening as a Diamond member who rarely shares negative feedback
While the hotel itself was decent — especially the lobby bathrooms (all the bathrooms are equipped with bidets) — my experience was marred by significant issues
Upon arrival, I was greeted by buckets placed throughout the lobby, catching water from a leaking ceiling
This unexpected situation led to a halt in check-ins
Although we waited two hours for the water to be restored, we soon discovered that there was no hot water available for our showers
For two days, my family and I endured freezing cold showers, despite being repeatedly assured that the issue was resolved and they were waiting for the boilers to heat up

I was visiting NYC with my family but my daughter fell very ill after taking 2 freezing cold showers so we were stuck in this dungeon for most of the trip and had to order medicine to help her recover
The lack of communication from the staff was particularly frustrating; we only learned about the hot water problem by stepping into the cold shower ourselves
On the third day, hot water returned, but it was scalding hot — so much so that it felt like boiling water
To add to our difficulties, the elevator broke down later that same day
When it came time to check out on the fourth day, I had to make three phone calls just to receive a copy of my bill via email
Given these numerous issues, I cannot recommend this property
I will unequivocally refrain from returning, unless points are offered in exchange for this review
I believe my experience warrants a serious reconsideration of management practices at this location
The hotel should change its name to Hilton Club The Central Motel — because that’s essentially what it is
There’s no food service of any kind, no stocked minibar (aside from two bottles of water), and just one ice machine for the entire hotel
Checkout is 10:00 am which is definitively a first
During our stay, there was no hot water in the shower for two full days
For $340 a night, you expect a lot more
Definitely avoid
Update! 8/2/2025

Now we have NO water at all! No guests where notified that the water was off! Another day in NYC without a shower or bath in the summer time!!! And with no notifications! Poor communication to guests, horrific experience!

We have been Hilton Grand Vacation owners for several years but this was the first time using out points to stay in NYC
We were lucky enough to snag a reservation over Labor Day weekend for a One Bedroom Penthouse room

I must say we were impressed! The room was beautiful and with the PH we had a fantastic view! Everyone mentions the heated toilet seat and bidet and of course I must too! What a nice added feature! But we also really enjoyed the power blinds and even the "Master" switch at the door so you don't have to turn everything off individually before you leave the room
Plus, especially for NYC, it was spacious! We had plenty of room for two adults and two kids
The staff was friendly and helpful as well
On the first night when we realized we did not have bedding for the sofa bed, we called to the front desk and not only did they send it right up, but they sent someone up to make up the bed for us
This was super helpful and so much nicer than the normal quick knock and dum of a pile of bedding into your arms at most hotels
The only reason I did not give 5 stars is for two reasons
First is that we arrived pretty early in the day (around 9am) and, as expected, our room was not close to ready
No problem, we expected that
We checked in, left our bags will bell services and off we went to explore
When we came back around 3:45pm with two highly exhausted girls, however, our room was STILL not ready

Of course, check in is not until 4:00pm so technically it was not late, but I guess I figured since we had been there so early that perhaps our room could have bene prioritized? This is something I don't understand about hotels
They will have rooms cleaned that sit empty for hours until the guests arrive while in some other cases, the guests are there early and they are made to wait
So we had to wait in their interior courtyard (which was very cute) and our room was FINALLY ready at 4:10pm
Second reason for 4 stars (versus 5) relates to trash pickup
Staying here as an owner, we do not get daily housekeeping, which we are fine with
But on the second day, I texted down to the front desk to ask what we should do with our trash that was piling up
I was told to just leave it outside our room
So we put it out there, assuming it would be picked up soon
24 hours later it was STILL sitting outside our room in the hallway
For such a nice place, this just seems a bit unseemly
So they could have been better about that
Overall, it's probably nit picking
We really did have a great time and would stay there again
Location is fantastic, rooms are nice and staff is exceptional (if they could just be better about trash)

My check for cleanliness starts with the on/off letters on light switches (the Central uses toggle switches), and there is no grim or dirt anywhere to be found
Everything is featuristic such as the alarm clock that is integrated with the phone and charges the TV remote (also plugs with USB and thunderbolt)
There are plenty of outlets, so you shouldn't need a power strip
There are even connections for USB-C on the desk

The rooms are NY sized, but has lots of storage so there is no clutter

Our stay at Hilton Wilmington Christiana had its ups and downs
Upon arrival, we were greeted warmly by the reception staff and promptly checked in (even a little early), which set a positive tone for our stay
The hotel offered a pleasant ambiance; the common areas were clean, and the lobby was well-appointed
Parking was ample and the location was convenient, offering easy access to local attractions, shopping areas, and the venue where we attended a wedding during our stay
Our initial room appeared well-maintained and comfortable
We did request a room with a lanai, which was lovely, but a room next to the pool can be noisy
The real problem arose when we attempted to take a shower
The water temperature fluctuated constantly, oscillating between scalding and freezing
We contacted the front desk twice, and they were quick to respond
However, the first technician insisted that the water was at a consistent temperature, and we simply didn’t let the water warm up long enough
On the second call, another technician named Stephen validated our complaint and offered us a room change without hesitation

It is always an inconvenience to have to switch rooms in the middle of a stay, but the second room did provide a respite from the inconsistent water temperatures, albeit now we had to endure the traffic noise from the other side of the hotel
Despite this issue, the hotel staff remained friendly and attentive, and the other aspects of our stay were satisfactory
We also really enjoyed the Market Bar and Restaurant
My only other complaint would be that for the price (nearly $800 for a 3-night stay), breakfast should have been included
Overall, it has potential, but improvements are needed to justify the cost

The Hilton Garden Inn New York/Midtown Park Ave is a stylish hotel located in the heart of Midtown Manhattan, just a short walk from the Empire State Building, Macy's, Fifth Avenue, and Times Square
It is the ideal base for business and leisure travelers alike, offering easy access to all that New York City has to offer

The hotel features oversized guest rooms with contemporary amenities, including complimentary WiFi, an ergonomic desk, a 32-inch HDTV, a walk-in shower, a Keurig® coffee maker, a microwave, and a mini-refrigerator
Four spacious terrace rooms are also available, offering wonderful views of NYC
Guests can enjoy a variety of delicious dining options at the hotel, including the Ainsworth Midtown restaurant, which serves breakfast, lunch, and dinner
The hotel also has a 24-hour business center and a fitness center, so guests can stay productive and active during their stay
Whether you are in New York City for business or pleasure, the Hilton Garden Inn New York/Midtown Park Ave is the perfect place to stay
With its convenient location, comfortable accommodations, and thoughtful amenities, the hotel offers everything you need for a great stay

With numerous locations worldwide, including major cities and popular destinations, it's a reliable choice for both business and leisure trips
Upon entering, guests are greeted by a welcoming atmosphere and a well-designed lobby
The rooms are well-appointed, featuring comfortable beds, ergonomic workspaces, and modern technology to enhance the stay

During my recent stay at the hotel, I encountered a few aspects that left me with mixed feelings
Firstly, the AC in the room was set at a fixed temperature of 74 degrees, and unfortunately, it couldn't be adjusted to a colder setting
As someone who prefers a cooler environment, this was a disappointment
It's important for hotels to cater to varying temperature preferences to ensure optimal comfort for all guests
In addition to the AC issue, the room itself felt somewhat dated
While it provided the basic amenities needed for a comfortable stay, the overall decor and furnishings could benefit from a modern touch to enhance the ambiance and create a more inviting atmosphere
Moving on to the conference room, which happened to be located in the basement, it too shared the same dated aesthetic as the guest room
Furthermore, the lighting in the conference room was noticeably low, creating a somewhat dim and less productive environment
Considering its basement location, introducing natural lighting or improving the overall lighting system would greatly enhance the functionality and appeal of the conference room
On a positive note, I must commend the staff for their pleasant and accommodating demeanor
Their friendly and helpful nature added a positive touch to my overall experience at the hotel
Their professionalism and willingness to assist guests are commendable qualities that significantly contribute to creating a welcoming atmosphere

In summary, while there were certain areas that could benefit from improvement, such as the inability to adjust the room temperature, dated room decor, and lackluster conference room lighting, the hotel staff's pleasant demeanor served as a redeeming factor
It's crucial for hotels to continuously evaluate and update their facilities to meet the evolving needs and expectations of their guests
If you prioritize a cool room temperature, a modern ambiance, and well-lit conference spaces, it may be worth considering alternative options
However, if friendly and helpful staff is a priority for you, this hotel might still provide a satisfactory stay
Ultimately, it's important for hotels to address these areas for improvement to ensure a more enjoyable experience for their guests

I stayed at the DoubleTree by Hilton Fort Lee (Reservation: R5974830004), and unfortunately my experience was extremely disappointing from the first night

I tried calling housekeeping several times with no answer
The front desk told me he was the only one available and that housekeeping had already left, and he could only give me clean pillow covers if I came down to pick them up myself
I ended up leaving the hotel that night and staying with a friend because the room was not in a clean or usable condition
Later during my stay, on November 17, the water in the hotel was completely shut off from 12 PM to 3 PM
We were only informed about this issue around 11:30 AM, which gave almost no time to prepare
This was extremely inconvenient and unacceptable for a hotel in this category
Additionally, the towels in the bathroom were another issue
Even after housekeeping “changed” them, the towels still had stains and visible marks on more than one occasion
I had to request new towels multiple times, and it felt like the hotel either has insufficient laundry capacity or simply needs to replace its towel stock entirely
Overall, this hotel does not meet the standards expected from a Hilton-branded property

Nice room and bathroom

The location of the Hilton Club West 57 TH street is very good
First and foremost, in relation to the wonderful Central Park, but also in relation to e
subway, short distance to shopping on 5 TH Avenue, for example
The room (we had a "King Studio") on the 14th floor with a view of 57th street, was also very good
We were very happy with the room itself
Very good and comfortable beds including pillows and mattresses
Everything was clean and nice, as well as a great bathroom in addition
As I see it, the cleaning staff did the best job and gave the best impression
They were both good and nice, all credit to them
Another advantage of this hotel was that there were never problems with crowded elevators, slow elevators and long waits for elevators, as you often find in more traditional hotels in NYC
However, the lobby area was not particularly inviting and pleasant
The lobby area was small and cramped and no seating where it was possible to sit secluded to relax or to do a little work
But what was most disappointing about the hotel was the general level of service and customer service from the reception staff
My assessment is that the level of this important element is that it was far below what I expect and what I am used to
I have stayed at other more traditional hotels nearby and further down towards Times Square, where the level of service was of a slightly different caliber
Not least, I have stayed at other Hilton hotels, especially on the west coast, where the customer service was at a significantly higher level
It started already at check-in, where they initially were unable to find my booking (this was sorted out relatively quickly)
When I, as politely as I could, asked if it was possible to get a "nice room a little higher up the floors", I received answer that "all our rooms are nice"

I mean, when you are going to stay at the hotel for a week and pay a significant amount of money for just that: it must be possible to at least try to be a little nice to the guests? How difficult is it to say, “thank you for choosing to stay here with us, have a wonderful week here at Hilton Club”?
A little more focus on customer service and maybe a few more hours on basic staff training, would hardly hurt
Another small challenge that I experienced was about my wish for a late check-out on the day of departure
We are talking about 3-4 hours extra time beyond the normal check-out time
This was anything but easy, even though I of course made it clear that I would pay for these extra hours
I also reffered to the hotel's own policies, where it was written in black and white that late check-out is possible
But anyway, this was sorted out in a good way in the end
But not without the hotel manager having to decide about it
The manager was nice and as mentioned, this was resolved
But I think this is one of very few times in my long hotel career (as a guest) that I have experienced late check-out as a real problem
But to summarize: the room and location were great, the cleaning staff were both nice and good, but beyond that I feel that Hilton Club West 57 TH is lacking to much in other areas
Therefore, it is important to be aware that Hilton Club is basically a slightly different concept than what you may be used to from other, more traditional hotels
This is important to be aware of BEFORE you book this hotel
I for one had not done my homework here, I had not done enough research beforehand
Therefore, there will probably be no return to the Hilton Club on my next trip to NYC, unless the hotel management convinces me otherwise

I bought a Hilton Grand Vacations time share here 3 years ago, and I've never been sorry

For me, that's dinner, and all the food is catered from a nearby & very tasty restaurant
If you don't own at this property, those meals won't be available to you, but there are many restaurants within easy walking distance
Staff have always been friendly, and the area very convenient
There's a Duane Reade on the east side and a wonderful grocery store a few doors west, and as stated, Carnegie Hall on the same block, at 57th & 7th Ave
This property is closer to the 6th Ave side
There's also a gymn, if you don't get enough exercise walking
For me, walking 3-5 miles a day is easy in the city
All in all,  it isn't cheap, but it's a great stay!

I stayed at the Hilton Club West 57th in NYC
I was very impressed
I did attend an informative session regarding ownership
I did not purchase at this time due to conflicting priorities, however I was satisfied with the information shared and it’s still a consideration for a later time
I loved that this building was located on Billionaire’s row in Manhattan

The building is very well kept and everyone was friendly

My room was on the 18th floor and had great views from the floor to ceiling windows
This review may not be applicable for everyone, but I think it is important to share because others may have (or be able to avoid) a similar experience
My partner and I were considering purchasing a timeshare with Hilton
We spoke with someone when we were staying in Hawaii, and purchased a travel plan to see how the program works

We were told by the person who sold the program to us (and received a commission) that the timing could be flexible and not to worry about the expiration on the contract because customer service could extend the time for us easily
I was concerned because the timing of expiration on the contract aligned with when I was scheduled to take the bar examination after completing law school
We really wanted this trip as a celebration for completing law school and the bar
I vocalized this to the agent and they assured me it would not be a problem

However, once the contract was signed, and we spoke with the customer service agents over the phone (no longer the people trying to sell us something) we were told, rather coldly that we would have to book the trip before the expiration time or forfeit the money
We were forced to book a whirlwind trip here in February, weeks before I am scheduled to take the bar examination, or lose the money we contributed to this "program
" We were forced to go to New York because this program only allows for a three day trip at this one location (even though they advertise dozens of other locations)
We previously stayed in Cabo for our first trip on this "program," which allows for a total of 7 days collectively for the credit purchased
We stayed in Cabo for four days and only had three days left under our program
Then we were told we could only stay in New York because that is the ONLY location that allows for three day stays
Absolute scam
We were (AND ARE) extremely upset with Hilton and their abysmal customer service, but decided to make the most of the trip rather than forfeit the money we already spent
However, it was clear upon arrival that we were not high on the priority list
We already had our "pitch" regarding the possibility of purchasing a timeshare in Cabo, which we declined
Our second stay in New York had no pitch scheduled and once the staff realized we were not a possible commission, the service became laughable
Also, we came when it was not busy, yet somehow ended up in probably one of the worst rooms possible, a courtyard room on the fourth floor, right next to the elevator
Also, this "view" of the court yard is an extremely generous statement
Upon checking in, I asked about the rooftop courtyard, and the receptionist responded, "that's for owners with a penthouse only," and made me feel like I was an idiot for even asking
Even though there are dozens of pictures on these reviews and the website showing a rooftop courtyard that looks public
So do not expect to be looking out at the city like in their picture advertisements, the view from our stay will likely be more indicative of your future experience
Needless to say, we will not be returning
I spent 7 days at this hotel and received world class service
Shout out to Sheila (reception) and Avi (door man)
They were so gracious and wonderful that we want to take them on our next vacation

I’ll start with the positives: the hotel has an excellent location and the breakfasts are really tasty
Unfortunately, the rest of the stay was quite disappointing
At check-in, we were told there had been a leak in the room and that there was a stain on the carpet
That wasn’t a huge issue for us
But the room itself was very small — especially around the beds
Looking at the floor plan, other rooms seemed to have a much better layout
Bottled drinking water wasn’t replaced in the room
When we asked, we were told it was “complimentary” — but that didn’t explain why it wasn’t restocked
The TV remote didn’t work — but to be fair, it was replaced quickly
The sink drain was stuck shut — also fixed promptly
Towels weren’t changed until we specifically requested it
When the shampoo ran out, they first brought shower gel and just left it on the bed
After asking again, they replaced it with shampoo — but never refilled the dispenser in the shower, even after several days of housekeeping
The wardrobe for coats is in the far corner of the room, so you can’t hang your jacket by the entrance — you have to walk through the whole room first
Overall, the room was very dusty — thick dust on the safe, wardrobe, and microwave, which clearly hadn’t been wiped in a long time
Great location and breakfast, but the cleanliness and attention to detail really let this hotel down

However water did not slope and drain to stay in the slippery shower floor (not ADA compliant)
No, the water slipped out and into the overall bathroom floor

The Hilton Garden Inn in Lower Manhattan is a great hotel and I would absolutely stay here again
The location is right across the street from the Staten Island Ferry and a 15-ish minute walk to the pathway across the Brooklyn Bridge
It is a skip over to so many cool spots in Lower Manhattan
The room was quiet, had all the things I needed (incl great water pressure!)
Elevators were fast enough and I never really had to worry about waiting too long (all times of the day)
There is a huge building blocking the center of most of the hotel views, but you get awesome views of the water on either side of it
I opted for a water view room and attached quite a few photos of the view from the 16th floor spot I was in
*It is worth noting that the bathroom door is not a door, but instead a sliding wooden door that doesn't necessarily give you complete privacy
*The window opens inward a few inches and has a sun shade in addition to the big, dark curtain
*Room had a mini fridge and micro
Fridge did NOT have a freezer
*Had an iron, no ironing board
Great location! Convenient access to the ferry

Had one of the worst hotel experiences at Hilton Garden Inn, New York

Day 2, we reminded the front desk and left for the day

Day 3, after a full day out, we returned to find that there was no shower gel in the room

As longtime Hilton Honors members, we’ve always booked our stays directly with Hilton
On one occasion, for a last-minute trip, we found a significantly better price on a third-party site
Before booking, we checked Hilton’s Price Match Guarantee and saw that they offered it
We carefully read the terms and conditions and confirmed that we met the requirements—everything was very clear
We called Hilton to request the match and were strung along for over an hour
Eventually, the representative told us they couldn’t get authorization to approve the match and transferred us
The next person’s system "went down" after about 20 minutes, and they hung up on us
We called back again, and after another 20 minutes, we were told that price matches couldn’t be approved over the phone—a direct contradiction of what the first agent had said
Then, we were transferred again to yet another department, where the agent couldn’t understand that a website might have a domain ending other than "
com
When we finally got to discussing the actual price, they began backtracking and claiming that the terms didn’t permit it
When I pointed out that I had read the terms, they responded that they were working off “different documentation
As a small business owner who goes out of their way to maintain goodwill with long-term customers, I’m done
I’ve wasted hours trying to follow the rules of a so-called guarantee that seems designed not to be honored
I will now share this experience with anyone considering this hotel chain
Ironically, they just posted a cut and paste reply below
So, they want me to waste more time to call them?  This is not a solution

The location is great- very close to the 1, R/W, 4/5 train stations
The Staten Island Ferry Terminal and the Battery Waterfront Park is right across the street
There are other terminals that’ll take you to IKEA and up to Williamsburg, which is great to tour the East River and offers great views of all three iconic bridges and the Manhattan Skyline
Many attractions are just one train ride away, such as Central Park, Times Square, the World Trade Center, the Rockefeller Center, Downtown Brooklyn, DUMBO, Empire State Building, Fifth Avenue, etc
Penn Station is also one train ride away, which is convenient as I took the Amtrak
I took a scenic walk along the waterfront about a block away and walked to the Brooklyn Bridge
The Charging Bull and the New York Stock Exchange is also a short walk away from the hotel
The location felt safe at night, which is relieving as I was traveling solo for the majority of the trip
The lobby had ample seating and outlets
The concierge was very helpful with checking me in
There were multiple elevators that were functioning properly and quickly
Ice machines are located on about every other floor
I didn’t eat at the restaurant/bar, but there is that option in case guests would like to settle in for a meal/drink
The gym is located in the basement and has a good amount of equipment and machines
Water bottles are provided
There is also a laundry room with multiple washer and dryer units
Wi-Fi is free and works throughout the hotel
Undoubtedly, many hotels in New York have smaller rooms, but I was pleasantly surprised on how much room I had to spread out my luggage and other belongings
I had a standard King Room
I didn’t feel cramped, and I brought a lot of stuff
The room felt a tad-bit dated and worn out, as there were a few scruffs on the furniture
I didn’t have a waterfront-facing room, however, I was still pleased as there was a lot of natural light coming from my view
The blackout curtains came in handy as I was still jet lagged for the first couple of days
Though, I do admit my room felt a little dimly lit at night
The wardrobe was small, but it did come with an iron and board
If I remember correctly, the mini-fridge in the room also came with a tiny freezer compartment
There is also a microwave, which really came in handy for warming up my tasty pizza and roti leftovers for breakfast lol
I liked the bathroom
The countertop was spacious, and the lighting was bright and ideal for skincare/makeup
There was a blowdryer, lotion, body wash, shampoo, and the shower had consistent hot water
The thermostat worked great for both heating and cooling
There’s a desk with ample outlets, an ergonomic desk chair and a lamp/desk light
The bed was very comfortable, though there admittedly was a couple of stained sheets the first night
Thankfully, the sheets were changed to cleaner ones throughout the rest of the stay
I arrived back late to transit delays
I couldn’t thank the staff enough for their understanding and professionalism, as they allowed me a little more time to pack up my belongings and clear out the room
The housekeeping staff was very friendly and helpful
Sadly, I couldn't remember her name, but one of the staff helped me take my bags out of the room and gave me a plastic bag to store and protect my big plushie 😂
I was grateful to leave my bags behind after checkout, which allowed me to explore the city for a few more hours worry-free
Overall, I enjoyed my stay here
It’s in a perfect location for easy access to Brooklyn, Staten Island, and the rest of Manhattan
The room is bigger than other hotels I’ve stayed in, and the staff were very helpful
The room was comfortable and spacious for me, and the area felt safe
I highly recommend staying here if you want a more affordable hotel in the Financial District/Lower Manhattan with a reputable hotel brand
Was an amazing stay in very neat rooms, and the views were amazing
Great place to stay if you are interested in Wall Street and everything lower NYC has to offer!

It is not

I recently stayed at the DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Newark Airport and experienced a very concerning issue with the water in my room
Both the hot and cold water from the sink and shower had a strong, musty, mold-like odor
The smell was not faint — it was immediately noticeable from several feet away as soon as the water was turned on
It was overpowering enough that it made using the sink or shower uncomfortable
To make sure it wasn’t just the drain, I performed a simple test by filling a cup with water and taking it into another room
The water itself still had a mild stagnant/musty smell when smelled up close, though not as overpowering as when running from the tap
This suggests a plumbing or maintenance issue rather than just a surface drain odor
This was not a sulfur or “rotten egg” smell — it was more like the smell of water left sitting in a bottle for days
Regardless of the cause, this is not what guests expect from a Hilton-branded property
I am not sure whether this is isolated to my room or a broader building issue, but it raises concerns about maintenance and sanitation standards
I would strongly encourage hotel management to investigate the plumbing system and ensure that guest rooms are being properly maintained

My three-night stay at this hotel was a profound disappointment and an incredibly frustrating experience, especially for an establishment advertised as 4-star under the Hilton DoubleTree banner
The hotel appears to be relying on its name while neglecting basic maintenance and service
Deceptive and Extremely Dated Room
The promotional pictures are misleading
The room I was assigned was extremely old and run-down:
The bedding was yellowish, a clear sign of poor cleanliness and neglect
The television was an archaic model using RCA cables (the 3-color cables), and the staff absolutely refused to allow me to plug in my HDMI cable (a first in modern hospitality!)
The bathroom's condition was shocking: a warped door, peeling paint, and overall decay
The beds were ridiculously small, the size of a teenager's bed, and not suitable for adult comfort
Non-Existent Housekeeping Service
This was the most unacceptable point: housekeeping was never performed during our three nights
I requested service at the front desk, and I was assured it would be done the next day, but we returned late to find full trash bins, missing towels, and the floor uncleaned
I had to go down to the reception to ask for soap and shampoo, as the bottles had been virtually empty since my arrival
This is not a 4-star service; it's self-service
Noise and Unpleasant Atmosphere
The soundproofing is catastrophic
I could distinctly hear conversations and noises from neighboring rooms, as if they were right next to us
Resting was impossible
The general atmosphere was unpleasant: dirty food on the floor in the elevators, unsupervised children in the gym, and screaming in the hallways that lasted until midnight
The parking lot was also completely overcrowded
Clumsy and Invalidating Service Recovery
Upon check-out, I raised all these issues
The only gesture offered was to waive a single night's parking fee, which felt like a standard procedure to quickly dismiss complaints
When I asked if that was all they had to offer, the agent responded by asking if I was a Hilton member, and what my tier was
This response implied that the validity of my concerns was contingent upon my status and spending with Hilton, which is unacceptable and highly insulting
The only positive aspect was the airport shuttle, which was on time, although capacity was often limited for the number of guests
Front desk staff were polite in their tone, but the service failures (housekeeping) prove that their kind words do not translate into action
Conclusion: This hotel is an insult to the DoubleTree brand

However, there were several downsides

In-room dining was inconvenient — there was no menu in the room (not even a digital one), so I had to go downstairs just to place an order
The location is also a bit isolated, with very few nearby restaurants or delivery options

The worst part of my stay was the experience at the restaurant on the first floor
One male waiter was extremely rude and dismissive

I recently stayed one night at the DoubleTree by Hilton Hotel Newark Airport while in town for a concert at the Prudential Center
While there were some positive aspects to this stay, there were also several issues, including a serious safety concern that I feel are worth sharing
Positives:
* Check-in was smooth and the lobby felt bright, open, and clean
* The breakfast was excellent, honestly one of the best I’ve had at any DoubleTree
Even with a large crowd on a busy Sunday morning, the dining staff handled everything efficiently
* The fitness center was clean, and the pool area was large and well-lit
I did not use the pool, but it did look very fun
* Elevators were fast and I never had to wait long
Concerns:
* Upon entering my room, I discovered a broken piece of metal lodged in the outlet near the bed, a serious safety hazard, especially for families with kids
* The mini fridge was stuck shut, and I didn’t want to force it open for fear of being put at fault for the unusable fridge
* The blinds were stained, and the windows were discolored with brown residue
* The door frame was damaged, which gave the room a worn, poorly maintained feel
* The thermostat was stuck at 67°F and couldn’t be adjusted, leaving the room uncomfortably chilly
Unfortunately, my experience at check-out was disappointing
Despite standing in the designated Diamond/Gold priority line, I was passed over multiple times while others in the regular line were helped ahead of me
I was never acknowledged or greeted until another guest in line invited me to go ahead
It was disheartening to feel ignored, especially as a loyal Hilton Diamond member and multiple staff at the front desk
I still appreciate the hard-working staff and the positive aspects of the property, but this stay fell short of the standard I’ve come to expect from Hilton
I hope these issues, especially the safety hazard, are addressed for the sake of future guests
I tried to contact the hotel directly about the concerns and was left in silence after 9 days

I would consider looking elsewhere for my future stays in Newark considering the radio silence I was dealt when trying to privately deal with my concerns
All photos are attached to this review including the stained window and blinds and the metal stuck in the outlet

My husband, who has Type 1 diabetes, and I, along with our two children, stayed at this hotel from July 20th to July 24th
On Tuesday night, I tried to order in-room dining for my husband since his blood sugar was low enough to warrant a meal, even though it wasn’t dangerously low at the time
However, I noticed that there were no menus in the room
I scanned the bar code on the desk to access the menu online, but unfortunately, there wasn’t one available
I then called room service to inquire about the menu, but they informed us that they had recently received a new menu and didn’t have a digital menu available
I explained our situation and requested a paper menu so that I could order my husband’s food
However, they informed me that there were no paper menus in the room, which was already evident
I asked if a menu could be brought up to us, but they denied that request
I asked if they could read off the menu to me so that I could order, but they also denied that request
They said that if we wanted a menu, we would have to walk downstairs to the bar, which defeats the purpose of in-room dining
This was also not feasible with two young children and a husband experiencing low blood sugar
Frustrated, I called the front desk to speak with a manager
I was informed that a manager was unavailable at 9:30 pm and that they couldn’t accommodate our request to bring a menu
As a result, we never got a chance to order any food
We ended up buying a soda from the vending machine on our floor to treat my husband’s low blood sugar and then ordered Uber Eats to be delivered to us
The next day, Wednesday, we visited the bar and requested a menu to bring to our room
The manager, at least I believe she was the manager, carried herself rudely when I asked for the menu
I had explained what had transpired the previous night, but she seemed uninterested and essentially dismissed it as the hotel’s standard practice
She kept interrupting me while speaking, showing no concern for the situation
During our stay, she offered two options for ordering food: either with her downstairs or whoever was at the bar
They would charge a 22% gratuity plus an additional $5 for the hassle of taking it upstairs
Alternatively, we could simply order the food and take it up ourselves
Our experience with housekeeping was equally disappointing
When we asked for toilet tissue, upon checking into our room we found only a half-roll in the restroom
We called and requested more, only to be informed that there was already toilet tissue in our room
We had to explain that it was only a half-roll and that there were four people in the room
After much back-and-forth, housekeeping finally arrived and brought a single roll of tissue to our room after waiting an hour
This experience was by far the worst I’ve had with Hilton
It’s understandable that it’s a sub-brand, but I hope they’ve improved their employee training and increased staff to provide in-room dining services if they don’t have enough staff to do so

We stayed at the DoubleTree by Hilton near Newark Airport and had a mixed experience
On the positive side, the front desk staff were friendly and helpful—offering complimentary essentials like toothbrushes and warm chocolate chip cookies, which my toddler loved
The hotel provides a convenient shuttle to and from Newark Airport every 30 minutes, which was a huge plus for our travel plans
The beds were also very comfortable, which made for a good night’s rest
However, the value didn’t quite match the price we paid
Breakfast was a steep $25 per person for a basic all-you-can-eat buffet—definitely overpriced for what was offered
Additionally, the on-site Starbucks couldn’t manage a simple cappuccino, which was disappointing
Overall, while the service and shuttle were appreciated, the amenities and pricing left us feeling like the stay didn’t quite measure up in terms of value

I stayed at this stylish New York City hotel recently and while it has serious curb appeal with its modern interiors and spotless rooms, there was one big letdown—no balcony, even though I booked a “balcony room
” That was a real disappointment
The overall ambiance was sleek and inviting, but the customer service felt a bit underwhelming and lacked the attentiveness you’d expect from a hotel in this price range
It has potential, but definitely needs improvement on the service front to match the visual appeal
Would I return? Probably not
But if you’re just looking for a clean, aesthetically pleasing place to crash without the bells and whistles (or a balcony!), it might still work for you
I was very disappointed with my stay
To start, the closest elevator to my room was broken, and because this is a very large hotel, I had to walk all the way across the building just to get to another elevator
It was inconvenient from the moment I arrived
What made the stay even worse was that there was a major music festival happening nearby—something I’m sure all surrounding hotels knew about and were preparing for
After spending the entire day at festival activities, I returned to my room around 3:00 AM exhausted, looking forward to a hot shower and some sleep
Instead, there was no hot water
I was barely able to wash up, and I couldn’t even wash my hair
After a long day, that was incredibly frustrating
The next issue was with a personal item I accidentally left behind—a magnifying mirror in the bathroom
I called the same day after checking out, while I was still in the area (about 10 minutes away), but no one answered the lost and found line
I then called the front desk, gave them my room number, and explained exactly where the mirror was
Instead of checking the room, they only checked the lost and found area and seemed uninterested in actually helping
I was told to call back the next day and that I’d have to pay to have the mirror shipped to me—even though I was nearby and could have picked it up myself
Other disappointments: breakfast is not complimentary, and there’s no microwave in the room, which would have been convenient
The only positive part of my stay was the woman at the front desk during check-in/check-out
She was kind, professional, and removed my breakfast charge after I told her about the cold shower, which I appreciated
It’s a shame, because this festival happens every year, and hotels in the area should be prepared for guests during such a busy time
Next year, I’ll make sure to book earlier—and stay somewhere else

We visited the Hilton Parsippany for a pop culture convention, so this review is about that experience not the rooms or the accommodations
As a convention venue, the Hilton Parsippany does a fantastic job with the space
Being a hotel, the layout is very open, modern, and easily navigable
Of course, plenty of parking, too
The layout of the meeting rooms provides plenty of space for vendors, celebrity photo ops, and the hoards of people attending the event
While the areas do fill up with shoppers and people trying to get from point A to point B, it all moves rather well
Attending a convention, regardless of size, at the Hilton Parsippany is a great experience
Navigating where you are and where you need to be is a breeze
The Hilton already has plenty of signage for the various rooms, and the convention added additional signage specific to their event
There is plenty of lighting and high ceilings in most of the spaces
The hotel staff keeps the communal areas clean and swept

During my stay, I had a discussion with the Front Office Manager regarding the AED 500 security deposit, despite my room being fully paid
Instead of explaining the policy professionally, the manager made a shocking and offensive remark:
“You can’t afford it
This comment was condescending, disrespectful, and completely inappropriate for anyone representing a hotel under an international brand like Hilton
Questioning a deposit policy does not justify insulting a guest or making assumptions about their financial capability

What makes this worse is that I am personally aware of previous guests (including family members) who stayed at this property and have still not received their security deposit refunds for over a year
This history makes guest concerns entirely valid and should be handled with transparency and respect — not arrogance
This experience reflects poor customer service, lack of professionalism, and weak management standards
A hotel of this caliber should train its staff to communicate respectfully and uphold the brand’s reputation
I do not recommend this property based on my experience

I am currently staying at Hilton Dubai The Walk, and the stay overall has been comfortable and enjoyable
The rooms are spacious, clean, and well-maintained, providing a relaxing environment for families
However, the main concern during this stay has been the breakfast and dinner arrangements at Hilton Dubai Jumeirah
As guests from Hilton The Walk are directed there for meals, the dining areas become significantly congested, especially during peak hours
Finding a table can be difficult, queues are often long, and the overall experience feels crowded rather than relaxing
The situation clearly reflects a gap in planning and resource allocation
While the staff are extremely polite, professional, and working very hard, they appear understaffed for the volume of guests being handled
This puts unnecessary pressure on them and impacts the guest experience

The administration should take a more proactive role in managing this—either by reopening additional dining facilities at Hilton The Walk or by increasing staffing and capacity at Hilton Jumeirah during busy periods
Better crowd management and operational planning would greatly improve the overall experience
In summary, while the rooms and staff are excellent, the congestion during breakfast and dinner needs urgent attention from management to match the standards expected from the Hilton brand

We had a very pleasant stay at the Hilton Dubai The Walk

First and foremost, we would like to say a massive thank you to Eloisa at the front desk, who was incredibly helpful with our accommodation and arranged everything for us perfectly and with a great attitude
The hotel breakfasts are simply awesome – there is a huge selection, but the absolute highlight for us was their delicious Eggs Benedict! The walk to the beach is very short
You just cross through the sister hotel opposite, and it's perfectly fine to go over wearing just your swimsuit and a t-shirt
The beach itself and the main pool are very well equipped
A huge plus is that we never had an issue finding available sun loungers and an umbrella, even when arriving after 10 AM
The beach is private and monitored by the staff, which keeps it peaceful
The sea was lovely and the water gets deep quite quickly, which is absolutely ideal for swimmers
The location is fantastic
Right on the main street in front of the hotel, there is an almost endless array of restaurants
Over our six days, we dined somewhere different every evening, and the food was always excellent
Furthermore, it's an easy walk from the hotel to the Dubai Marina area, which features a large shopping mall (Marina Mall)
If you don't mind a bit of walking, you can even reach the metro station on foot, so you definitely don't have to rely on taxis everywhere you go
Overall, we were extremely satisfied and highly recommend this hotel!

After a long flight we turned up at the hotel at about 1am, to find our room was no longer available and to be put in a ‘holding room’ which was of a lower standard to what we had prepaid
This was very disappointing and not something you’d expect from a Hilton hotel

Overall we experienced 3 rooms at this hotel for a 4 night stay, and can fairly say all the rooms were quite dated, they had very little sound proofing making all hallway noises very loud and it easy to hear anything going on in an adjacent room
In addition to this, all rooms had a door that gave access to an adjoining room, which we found unusual and even had someone in the other room tried to get into ours at 2am
The lifts were another challenge, often having to wait over 10 minutes until one finally arrived
This was mentioned by other people we met in the lifts and was even worse at busy times such as breakfast

When complaining to the main desk about the first night, they tried to suggest an appropriate recompense was £30, as we had paid significantly more than this per night, this was not only disappointing but dismissive of the impact moving rooms at 2am after a long flight had on us

Whilst this location was good for access to restaurants, we would note that from 10pm to the early hours the road is incredibly busy and loud, with cars showing off and revving engines throughout the evening
A taxi to the Dubai Mall was about £20 from here

Hilton The Walk is a rare example of a hotel that truly lives up to its prestigious reputation
The first thing that strikes you is the incredible view from the windows—a breathtaking panorama that defines the entire atmosphere of your stay
For food enthusiasts, the hotel offers access to multiple world-class restaurants
Each venue impresses with a vast variety of dishes, stunning scenery, and remarkably fast service
The location is a strategic masterpiece for any traveler
Staying here allows you to completely bypass the need for taxis or public transport
Everything you might need—from the beach to premium shopping—is within easy walking distance
The service is beyond reproach
I want to highlight Eloisa, whose professionalism is unmatched; she handles every request with incredible speed
The WhatsApp concierge service is another standout feature, providing instant support and a hassle-free experience
Whether you are here for business or a family vacation, this hotel is a perfect choice
The only minor drawback is the elevator wait time during busy periods, but in the grand scheme of things, it’s negligible
A solid 10/10 experience!

С первого дня было видно, что это настоящия профессионалы, которые относятся своей раddre полной отдачей и искренней заботой о постих||Данило и Хамид всегда были невероятно вежливыми, занимательными, добра желательными тетовыми помочь в любой ситуации независимо от сложности вопроса или времени обращения Они на просто выполняют свои обязанности, а действительно стремятся сделать пребывание максимально комфортным приятными запоминающимся||Любая просьба решалась, оперативна, с улыбкой и большим вниманием деталям Благодаря их опыту организованности и отличному знанию своего дела всегда чувствовалась уверенность и спокойствия редков и очень ценное качество сервиса

В первую очередь хочу предостеречь о том, что отель подает запросы  в Google maps,  если реальные посетители рассказывают свои истории и ставят заслуженный рейтинг/звезды (как можно понять, не пять звезд)

Отдыхала в этом отеле с 19 по 21 октября 2025 года
Ресепшен
Менеджер на ресепшен спрашивал паспорт моего мужа,хотя я объясняла, что отдыхаю одна , но бронирование делал муж и также указал в комментарии бронирования мое имя и фамилию
Информация обо мне у отеля была заранее
Думаю, что является не законным спрашивать оригинал паспорт  супруга , если тот не отдыхает совместно, а только делает бронирование
Странно (и не законно) брать на отдых паспорт чужого физ лица, если он не отдыхает
Заселение
Я приехала в 10-11:00 утра и оставила свой чемодан у консьержа
Номер был не готов
Далее я приехала в отель вновь уже в 14:00 -14:30 номер все еще был не готов
Предложили лаун на 35 этаже
В 14:40 подошедших менеджер и сообщил, что номер будет готов только через 40 минут, то есть примерно в 15:20
По итогу заселение было чуть ли не в 16:00, хотя по регламенту отеля заселение должно быть в 15:00
К слову, в лаунже на 35 этаже было только кофе из не очень чистой кофе машины и жутко сладкие соки, вода
Так же при заселении не рассказали включен ли завтрак, где он проходит, есть ли бассейн / тренажерный зал / бар и тд
В целом можно сказать, что персонал на ресепшен был не внимателен
Лифт
В отеле 3 основных лифта и 2 - для персонала
В первый день пребывания (19
25) очень сложно было с вызовом лифта
Вечером , чтобы спуститься с 10 этажа прождала около 7 минут
С чем это было связано, я не знаю
Отель сам по себе не новый
Для розеток требуется переходник
Завтрак
Более-менее приличный
Однако очень многолюдно, шумно
На входе останавливают и создается очередь
Хорошее месторасположение, если вас интересует пляж, рестораны, набережная
Из-за сложившейся ситуации с поздним заездом сделали поздний чек-аут (в 16:00) без доплат, но тоже не без дополнительных разговоров
