Pour commencer, l’hôtel est idéalement situé dans un quartier très animé, avec promenade le long de la mer, commerces, boutiques et restaurants
Ensuite, vous êtes à quelques minutes à pieds de la marina pour vous balader et 10-15 minutes à pied de la grande roue par le bord de mer
Pour aller à Burj Khalifa compter entre 30 à 50 minutes en taxi (selon l’heure à laquelle vous y aller)
L’hôtel, était super bien décoré, l’accueil, la gentillesse de l’ensemble du personnel est à souligner, que ce soit à la réception, au restaurant, à la piscine, la plage ou dans les étages
Le restaurant Oceana offre un buffet très varié le matin et le soir et un buffet spécial Noël le 24 et le 25 décembre a été proposé par l’hôtel, dans une très belle ambiance

Nous avions également accès au lounge, situé au 9eme étage, qui nous donnait accès à un petit-déjeuner (en plus du buffet principal, plus calme), à un tea-time et « apéro-Time »! 2 piscines sont disponibles, une réservée + de 16 ans, l’autre pour tous
L’accès à la mer se fait directement de l’hôtel
Notre chambre avait vue sur la grande roue, la mer et la promenade

Whenever there is a Hilton in the country I visit, I always prefer to stay at Hilton because I have always left satisfied
For the first time, I regret staying at a Hilton in Dubai
We paid for our one-week vacation before even arriving in Dubai because it was confirmed that we would stay in a 145 m² sea-view suite on the top floors
The only reason we booked such a large room is that we enjoy staying in rooms with a sea view; otherwise, we usually do not even spend much time in the room
Despite this, after checking into the hotel, we were given a room on the lower floors, located between buildings
When I informed the reception about the situation, the staff said they could only provide a room if we accepted the first floor
Is this why we pay such high fees before even arriving?
The Dubai Hilton family caused our vacation to start badly the moment we entered the hotel
Please do not mislead customers during reservations

We faced a disappointing experience during our recent stay
We had booked one of their penthouses for a weekend getaway with my sisters and our children, but upon check-in, our apartment was not ready
Unfortunately, there were no apologies offered; instead, the manager informed us that it was not her fault and she couldn't guarantee when it would be ready
She suggested an alternative room, which she referred to as "complimentary," although we had already paid for our booking, making the term confusing in this context
|While we understand that issues can arise during busy periods, the customer service and recovery efforts did not meet the standards expected from Hilton
It was disheartening to experience what felt like a lack of professionalism and understanding
At one point, we felt that we were not being treated unfairly and the offer of minimal service followed by a gesture of free food as compensation, which we declined, did not address our concerns
|Our primary issue was not just the inconvenience, but also the impression that the situation was not a priority for Habiba
However, we later spoke with Ayman, who listened to our concerns and offered a sincere apology, which was appreciated
We are considering returning to see if this was an isolated incident with Habiba or if it reflects a broader issue with the brand's service in the UAE

Despite being a Hilton Honors member, I faced multiple issues during check-in
After arriving from the airport, I was given a room different from what I had booked
When I got to the room, I was surprised to find it was not a king bed as reserved, but two separate beds
I tried calling the front desk, but no one answered
Exhausted from traveling, I went back down to reception, and they apologized, saying they had no room available matching my booking
Frankly, in serviced apartments or professionally managed properties, when this happens the guest is usually upgraded—not downgraded
And that wasn’t the only problem
I accepted the room temporarily because I was completely exhausted
When I used the bathroom, water started leaking everywhere
I called again—this time they answered—and I explained that the situation was unacceptable, as the water spread throughout the room due to very poor plumbing quality
After that, I was moved to another room that matched my original booking—despite being told just five minutes earlier that it wasn’t available
They apologized for this disaster and the lack of professionalism by offering breakfast, which I neither wanted nor could use since I don’t wake up in time for it
Other observations: for a five-star hotel, the “cost-cutting” approach is extremely disappointing
If you need basic amenities (ear plugs, a toothbrush, or any essential item), you are told to call—but when you call, no one answers
Also, the bottled water is served in glass bottles that appear to be refilled for “environmental reasons
” If sustainability is truly the goal, then provide sealed water from a recognized brand (e
, Nova, Berain, etc
), not reused bottles

Sono stato al Hilton Dubai Jumeirah nel vibrante quartiere di Jumeirah Beach Residence (JBR), proprio sulla famosa passeggiata The Walk, e per me è stata un’esperienza davvero piacevole e comoda
La posizione non potrebbe essere migliore: a due passi dalla spiaggia privata, circondato da negozi, bar e ristoranti, con vista diretta su mare e skyline moderno di Dubai Marina
La posizione è uno dei punti forti: si raggiunge facilmente la spiaggia privata, con lettini e ombrelloni puliti e il mare davanti è perfetto per fare una nuotata o rilassarsi guardando il tramonto
La struttura si trova proprio sul lungomare The Walk, perfetto per passeggiate, shopping e cocktail serali, e permette di esplorare tutta la zona a piedi senza troppi spostamenti
La camera in cui ho soggiornato era spaziosa, luminosa e confortevole, con tutti i servizi tipici di un Hilton: letti comodi, bagno ben fornito, pulizia impeccabile e vista mare/parziale sulla città
Anche il Wi-Fi gratuito e il comfort generale mi hanno soddisfatto
L’hotel offre piscine, spa e palestra di buon livello, e ha accesso diretto a molti ristoranti e bar vicini, così come a quelli collegati del resort gemello
La colazione è ricca e offre molte opzioni internazionali, ottima per cominciare la giornata prima di esplorare la città
Il personale è stato sempre cordiale, disponibile e professionale, pronto a dare indicazioni e consigli su attività e ristoranti locali
È un hotel di fascia alta ma non ultra-lussuoso come altri di Dubai, quindi il rapporto qualità-prezzo resta convincente

Per me il Hilton Dubai Jumeirah è una scelta eccellente se vuoi un soggiorno comodo, con accesso diretto alla spiaggia, posizione perfetta su The Walk e servizi ben organizzati, ideale sia per vacanze mare sia per esplorare Dubai con facilità

I had booked a room for a 4-night stay in Dubai

My wife and I were celebrating our 25th wedding anniversary! During the app-based check-in process, I selected a room, which unfortunately was not available when we reached the hotel because the guests staying therein had requested a late check-out
Regardless, the hotel staff arranged for an alternate room on the same floor with superb views of downtown Dubai and the creek
Before giving us the room, the hotel staff decorated the room for our milestone anniversary and also baked us a cake - gestures we truly appreciate!
The King studio room was immaculate and came with a kitchen complete with a 4-seater round dining table, stove, oven, refrigerator, and a sink
The room also housed a washer-dryer, which we did not use
Additionally, there was ample storage space for our four large bags
The furniture and bed in the room were cozy and comfortable
The staff responsible for room cleaning were professional and courteous, maintaining the room in excellent condition
On the other hand, the water pressure in the bathroom was quite low, making the rain shower head, and the handheld shower, essentially anemic
A feature that we found somewhat odd was the placement of the flat screen TV in the middle of the room between the bed and sofa, effectively serving as a divider
Perhaps the TV could have been wall-mounted, but this was not a major concern since we were mostly out and about in the city
Regardless, I tried connecting my phone to the TV using the 'cast' function but was unsuccessful
I am tech savvy person, but the interface on the TV to successfully 'cast' content, left a lot to be desired
I've stayed in many Hilton hotels but never experienced such an inability to cast content from my devices
Next comes the food! While we did not eat in the hotel's only restaurant, the morning breakfast spread was quite sumptuous
There was the usual continental fare including breads, cheeses, and juices in addition to local dishes such as hummus and pita, tabouleh salad, olives and feta, a soup with fava beans, and the like
Since we are vegetarian, we did not check out the meats and such
On one of the days, we were there, the breakfast buffet included poha - an Indian staple which was well-prepared and served hot
Further, the kitchen could also prepare additional items from a limited selection
One of the days, we ordered aloo parantha and on another day we ordered idli and vada with sambhar
The aloo parantha was well-made, but the idli/vada was just passable
Overall, our stay was comfortable, restful and relaxing

The hotel staff, including Riki at the reception, Ahmed in guest services, and Malika - Guest Services Ambassador, were eager to help us and make sure we felt welcomed! I want to convey my sincere thanks to the entire hotel staff, and particularly to Riki, Ahmed, and Malika

We chose this hotel and booked a two-bedroom apartment for our business trip of three people
First of all, we would like to express our gratitude to RIKI, the front desk receptionist
Since we were unsure about the end time of our trip, we had to extend our stay on short notice several times
He was extremely patient in rearranging rooms for us to minimize any inconvenience, and his service was both professional and thoughtful
We particularly liked the two-bedroom, three-bed unit
It featured a spacious wrap-around balcony and an oversized living room, which delivered an excellent living experience and stunning views
We also want to give a big thumbs-up to the housekeeping and room service team—they always delivered our requested items promptly, no matter the time of day
If we were to point out any inconveniences, there would be three aspects
First, we were not fond of the shower setup in the room, which requires you to stand inside the bathtub while bathing; to our mind, this is neither very safe nor hygienic
Second, this is a very personal observation, but the perfume scent in the hotel lobby was quite strong and overpowering, which we found hard to get used to
Third, some rooms lack a desk lamp, leaving no well-lit space for working (especially if you do not wish to work at the dining table)
Overall, we were quite satisfied with our stay and would be happy to choose this hotel again if we have the chance

During my recent stay at the Hilton Dubai Creek Hotel & Residence I was impressed from the very beginning
Before arriving I received a pleasant welcome email providing helpful information about the property and its facilities which I found useful
On arrival I was greeted by the friendly team in the unusual parking area entrance which felt a little different but worked well
I made my way to reception on the ground floor and was again welcomed and supported by the team
Check in was quick and efficient and before long I was on my way to my room
On arrival I was pleasantly surprised to find a lovely apartment style room with a nice balcony overlooking the creek and hotel pool
The apartment included a kitchen and dining area, a separate bedroom and a separate bathroom and toilet with all the usual amenities
The property offers the usual Hilton facilities including a nice restaurant, Shisha bar, spa, gym and a relaxing pool area with a bar available too
There was also a happy hour in the restaurant for Hilton loyalty members which was a nice touch
It is worth noting the hotel is just over a year old and the unique entrance  can create some confusion for taxi drivers
I found that walking to the hotel next door helped as it has a more traditional hotel entrance which made it easier for drivers to locate
The residence is part of a lovely development on the creekside and the newly opened supermarket close by was ideal for picking up essentials or for a simple meal in the apartment
The location is convenient for both the airport and the original parts of Dubai which makes it a great base for exploring the city
Overall I really enjoyed my stay, the team were helpful throughout and the property was always clean and well maintained
I look forward to visiting again in the future

few points to consider when booking this property
CON - The map pinned on the website/app does not bring you to the hotel lobby
Access is through the basement which is difficult to navigate especially when you're a tourist in Dubai
Guest service is difficult to reach, there were times when my calls were disconnected without explanation
The water temperature in the master's bedroom is extremely difficult to regulate, either too hot or too cold, clearly requires a PhD to balance
Engineering sent to check didn't fix it
I had 4 room key cards, only one is working properly

PRO - Breakfast service by Tanmay is a standout! He's attentive, polite, and provides excellent service, definitely ask for him when dining at the property

The TV remote, the doorknob, telephone, the hangers, light switch, the curtains, the window handles, chairs, and the bed frame? Do you clean the walls, especially bathroom walls every time? The showerhead, the handles in the bath?

I loved the breakfast, a great mix of hot food and continental! Tea was nice! Staff were helpful! We ordered breakfast to the room one day and it was great! We got to choose what we wanted from the menu and hang it up outside for them to see our choices!
Only thing is, the whole point of wanting the breakfast up in our room was to get more sleep time

Staff called us at 9 am checking if we still wanted the room service which was a bit annoying! Also one of the days we went down to get breakfast and the table we were directed to was poorly laid, leaving us to gather cutlery and condiments from other tables
The room service was amazing, we had people bringing toiletry top ups
We did order dinner to our room and they brought it inside which was amazing!

For dinner we called them at 11 which is very late, and the only available option was a margarita pizza! It is fair because it was quite late, but the hotel might want to consider having more dinner options available for late night room service, especially as they advertise 24/7 room service
Also £10 for two small bottles of Coke is unjustifiable
The toilet has hand cream and hair conditioner, which is very thoughtful of them! It’s minor stuff like this, that says they care about having a high quality service!

I know having a fridge in the room is not something every hotel does in this country, but this is Hilton! Surely they can afford it! Also another minor thing was the toilet roll holder is poorly designed, the roll kept dropping from it as we used the bathroom!
But overall, the stay was lovely! Worth paying a little extra! Very lovely staff, nice location, nice rooms!
Please get back to me regarding my questions, thank you!

Hilton York, 1 Tower Street
Perfect location for a York stay

Walk around the side of the hotel and you’re right in the city centre: JORVIK Viking Centre is about 100 metres away, Fairfax House is roughly 50 metres, and Clifford’s Tower is practically on the hotel’s doorstep
You’re also surrounded by plenty of bars, restaurants, and shops
Our room was very well appointed and spotless, with a modern-classic feel throughout the hotel
It’s clearly well maintained, and our room’s view of Clifford’s Tower was amazing
The bar is modern and comfortable, and breakfast was a strong highlight—an extensive buffet plus a chef making eggs to order
Parking-wise, pay for the on-site valet
There’s no self-parking at the hotel, and the nearby Castle car park is expensive, so valet is the easiest, best-value option
Service was good overall—friendly and helpful—but not quite excellent; it could be a touch sharper and more responsive at times
That said, everything we needed was handled without fuss
Overall, highly recommended
We visit York a few times each year and would definitely stay here again for the unbeatable location, comfortable rooms, and solid amenities

I chose the DoubleTree by Hilton Birmingham Perimeter Park because I typically trust the Hilton brand
This stay did not meet even basic expectations
From the moment we arrived, the condition of the property raised concerns
The parking lot was visibly unkempt, with trash present, and there is a posted sign stating “park at your own risk” with no liability for loss or damage
At the same time, guests are required to pay for parking
Charging for parking without any visible security, patrol, or controlled access is difficult to justify
During check-in, I accidentally left my wallet at the front desk
After getting to our room, I realized it was missing and called the front desk multiple times with no answer
We went back downstairs, and the clerk confirmed he had my wallet
While I was relieved it was there, what followed was unacceptable
He had my wallet, knew exactly who I was and what room I was in, yet made no effort to contact me
I then had to ask him directly if he saw the incoming calls
His response was that he did see them and chose not to answer
There was no explanation and no urgency
Given the lack of communication, I had no choice but to assume the worst and immediately cancel my cards to protect myself
That added unnecessary stress and disruption to what should have been a routine check-in experience
We were also given incorrect information at check-in regarding breakfast
We were told it was included, but after eating on our second morning, we were informed it was not
To her credit, a staff member stepped in and handled the situation professionally
That was the only moment during our stay that reflected the level of service I expect from Hilton
The room itself showed signs of poor maintenance, including visible stains on the carpet
The TV connection was weird
Always disconnected from the service and multiple remotes
Additionally, there was a repeated police calls to the property, which was alarming and added to an already uncomfortable environment
Between the condition of the property, the lack of security despite paid parking, and the handling of a lost wallet, this stay fell well below expectations
I expect better standards, communication, and accountability from a Hilton-branded hotel
I would not stay at this location again, and I hope management takes this seriously and addresses both property conditions and staff

We arrived at the hotel at 10:30am CST
The woman at the desk looked at us and said, "I do not have any rooms available
Check-in is at 4pm
" We asked if she could pull our reservation so if a room became available she could notify us
She told us to download the app and when our room was available, it would notify us
We did and once logged in, the app told us to go to the desk to provide more info
The woman said, "It's probably because your room isn't available
" She basically waved us away and told us to come back at 4pm

5 hours later but she said no rooms were ready

" She seemed very annoyed by our request but it turns out there were rooms available because less than 2 minutes later we were going up to a room
The hotel was not busy

10/10 Experience at the Hilton Doubletree in Birmingham!
We had an amazing time at the Hilton Doubletree in Birmingham this weekend
The hotel itself is fantastic, but what really stood out was the incredible hospitality from the entire team
Not only did we enjoy the amenities, but we also connected with some wonderful people—everyone from the head chef and catering team to the bartenders and front desk staff
What made this visit so special was how quickly word spread about the kind comments we made about the excellent service
Before we knew it, staff members were coming out to introduce themselves, making us feel like VIPs

The service was so warm and genuine—you could tell they treated us as well as they’d treat the CEO of the company! Shoutouts go to Dave at the bar, Constantine at the front desk, Debra on breakfast duty, and the Friday night catering and Saturday restaurant teams (special thanks to the head chef)
The location couldn’t be better
Pappadeaux’s is right across the highway, and there are several unique restaurants within walking distance
The area is calm and feels very safe, with visible security and law enforcement nearby
This hotel is ideal for hosting events, weddings, or just enjoying a weekend getaway
The size of the venue is perfect, the atmosphere is welcoming, and you’ll meet a diverse range of guests
The staff and many of the patrons make everyone feel at home right away
To top it all off, the facility’s amenities are outstanding
Whether it’s the dining options, event spaces, or the overall environment, everything is thoughtfully designed to ensure a great experience
Thank you to the entire team at the Hilton Doubletree in Birmingham for making our visit so memorable
We highly recommend this place to anyone visiting the area—it’s a solid 10/10 from us! We’ll definitely be back

I stayed at The Kelly Birmingham, Tapestry Collection by Hilton while in town for a major career-related event January 17th -18th 2026, and unfortunately this was one of the most disappointing hotel experiences I’ve had
I arrived around 4:50 PM—nearly two hours after check-in time—and was told my room had not yet been cleaned and I would need to wait
I waited approximately 30 minutes, during which time several guests who arrived after me were checked in immediately and escorted to their rooms
The desk attendant even offered for us to sit at the bar for drinks, we declined as our time was limited
This delay put me behind schedule for an important event
After expressing my frustration, the front desk attendant offered a breakfast credit that required charging items to the room and trusting they would be credited back  after checkout
I declined and asked for a manager
I was told the manager would be available the next day
When I finally entered the room—the one I was told “needed to be cleaned”—it was clearly not clean
There was hair in the bathtub, on the sink, and on the bathroom floor
The shower/tub/toilet  leaked continuously, leaving standing water between the shower and toilet for the entirety of my stay
The carpet was sticky in places, turning the soles of our socks (and feet) black
The room temperature was 64 degrees upon entry while it was 30 degrees outside, and the heat took an unreasonable amount of time to come on
We did call to the front desk to report the issue, they advised to "give it a few minutes" and if it did not come on they would send someone up
It came on about 25 minutes later
Because of the issue with the room at check in, I was late to my event
The next morning, I again asked to speak with a manager and was told no manager was on site
The desk clerk (who was kind and professional) offered a $50 credit, asked me to leave my contact information, and assured me the manager would call me Monday
No one called Monday
Or Tuesday
I finally called the hotel on Wednesday and was directed to Kenyatta White
Initially, she seemed empathetic and stated that the situation should have been handled before reaching her
She told me she would process an additional refund and add Hilton Honors points, and I specifically requested a detailed email outlining the refund and points
She assured me it would be sent immediately
It was not
When I called back on Thursday, January 22, Ms
White had a noticeably smug and dismissive attitude
She claimed we had already discussed the refund and points and did not know "what the issue I had was" because it had been "handled"
The “refund” unknown to me was the original $50 credit from the front desk, the day of my checkout, not anything additional
She did not tell me how many points were added and coldly instructed me to check HiltonHonors
com myself
To make matters worse, the 1,000 points she claimed were added on January 21st did not appear until I followed up again on January 22nd , after my call with Mrs
White, and while I was writing this review
The lack of transparency and follow-through is extremely disappointing
I do feel i should have been compensated greater for this inconvenience
My career  and reputation was jeopardized due to a lack of coordination with this hotel
This entire experience—from check-in, to room conditions, to management’s handling of the situation to fruition—shows a serious lack of organization and accountability
I now understand why Hilton properties are no longer my first choice, and I would not recommend this hotel to anyone traveling for an important event

I recently stayed at The Kelly Birmingham as part of a business trip and, unfortunately, my experience was less than ideal despite the hotel’s friendly and professional staff
While I was greeted warmly by every team member I interacted with, the property’s condition did not meet the expectations I have for a Hilton Tapestry Collection hotel
The major issue I encountered was with the guestroom door lock, which malfunctioned throughout my stay
I had to make seven trips to the lobby to address the issue before maintenance was finally called
While the staff was courteous and accommodating, it took multiple attempts before they addressed my concern about the lock’s battery, and even then, the problem persisted for several days
Additionally, the bathroom was disappointing
The showerhead had visible buildup, indicating it hadn’t been properly cleaned in some time, and the water pressure was extremely low, despite a maintenance check that didn’t resolve the issue
On top of that, the body wash, which was not mounted on the shower wall like the shampoo and conditioner, was left on the ledge and nearly empty every day of my stay
I had to struggle just to get a small amount out each time, which was incredibly frustrating
While the staff provided excellent service and made me feel welcome, the maintenance and cleanliness issues made my stay less than comfortable
Given these challenges, I would hesitate to return for another business trip

stayed at the Hilton in Alpharetta for a work trip and overall had a solid experience with just a few areas that could use improvement
I arrived late—as usual—and check-in went smoothly
The front desk agent was extremely polite and helpful

I asked for a plastic key (I always do this as a backup in case the app fails), and while she was processing it, another employee (Not front desk maybe engineering are maintenance) chimed in to say I didn’t need to visit the desk if I checked in on the app
I had literally just explained why I came down, so that felt a bit dismissive
Either way, I got my keys (she gave me two) and headed up
My Hilton Gold status wasn’t acknowledged—not a big deal, but it’s usually part of the routine
My room was on the 7th floor and looked great
Just inside the door was a Cuisinart coffee maker (dual-cup), a small refrigerator with two complimentary water bottles, and a low countertop with two drawers
Between the coffee area and the closet was a wall mirror
The closet itself had hangers, an ironing board, iron, and a safe
At the top of the closet, there was what looked like a concealed compartment—unclear what it's for, but it stood out

The bedroom had a wall-mounted TV above a second set of drawers with an ice bucket and glasses, a desk with a non-rolling alligator print chair (surprisingly stylish), and a plush armchair with a side table and lamp
Bedside setup included two lamps, an alarm clock, and a limited number of USB ports and plugs
Outlets were scarce overall—one near the coffee maker (but half-blocked), and one in the hallway
The bathroom was clean, with a glass shower, lighted mirror, and a built-in nightlight (which I always appreciate)
Towels were average—not luxury, but not thin or scratchy
A hair dryer was provided
The door had a solid privacy guard at the bottom
I didn’t have much of a view at night, but the room felt comfortable and well-kept
Breakfast was a bit of a mixed bag
The staff was great—super friendly and welcoming—but the food didn’t impress
The bacon was practically inedible, hard like plastic
Pork sausage links were decent, and the Gouda cheese grits were flavorful but salty
Pre-made waffles were good
There were the usual extras like breads, pastries, and fruit, but overall it didn’t feel like a standout Hilton breakfast
I didn't go back after that for the rest of my stay
The snack shop by the front desk could use serious attention
It was heavily stocked with healthy options that didn’t seem to be selling, while candy and chocolate were gone almost immediately
I had a late night chocolate craving and came up empty-handed—only ice cream was available
On top of that, they sell glass Coke bottles that require a bottle opener, but there’s none in the room or the shop
I had to go back to the front desk and ask the guy to use the one he carries in his pocket
That shouldn’t be how it works—either include a bottle opener in the room or offer one with the purchase
I didn’t request housekeeping during my stay, but I liked that they left a card on my door letting me know I could reach out by 2 PM if I needed anything
That’s a nice, considerate touch I haven’t seen at other Hiltons
Parking is mostly in the rear
There are Diamond and handicap spots up front, but for most guests, you’ll need to walk a bit either to the front entrance or the side door—even from the closest spot in the main lot
Each evening, the hotel had a lively vibe
The bar and patio areas had groups hanging out, enjoying drinks and conversation
It seems like a fun place to stay if you're here for leisure
I was just in town for work, so I didn’t get to take advantage of it—and I never even saw the pool (if they have one)
The night staff were friendly and attentive every time I passed through the lobby
Overall, the hotel is clean, the staff is great, and the room was comfortable
Just a few tweaks—better breakfast execution, a stocked snack shop, and accessible bottle openers—would elevate the stay

There should have been 2 Food/Beverage credits on our room - 1 for each night - and each should have been for $30 per night (2 people, $15 per person per night)
Instead, only a single night was credited for the $30 and the second night was not credited
We assumed that this would be a very easy matter to get corrected, however we were mistaken
We contacted the hotel every day for 6 days, leaving messages that were not returned
Finally somebody at the hotel provided us with an email address and asked us to email the manager that deals with these things (initials MB - Accounts Receivable)
We did so and heard nothing at all
After 8 days of silence we again emailed, and then called

We spoke to Hilton corporate customer service who apologized immediately, confirmed that there should have been a credit for each night and the amount should definitely not have been paid and that they would escalate it

Review: The Starling Atlanta Midtown – Great Location, Disappointing Rooms
The only real selling point of The Starling is its location
It’s right in the middle of Midtown — walkable to Piedmont Park, Colony Square, and plenty of restaurants and bars
If you want convenience, it has that
Beyond that, it falls apart
The rooms are poorly designed and feel neglected
The décor tries to be bold and modern but comes off mismatched and cheaply executed
Layout choices don’t make sense, finishes feel worn, and the overall impression is dated rather than stylish
Cleanliness is the bigger issue
The room did not feel properly cleaned — dust, stained surfaces, and a general lack of attention to detail
Bathrooms in particular felt grimy rather than fresh
For a hotel at this price point, that’s unacceptable
The lobby and marketing suggest an upscale experience
The rooms do not deliver it
If location is your only priority, it works
If you care about thoughtful design, cleanliness, and overall quality, there are better options in Midtown

My wife and I stayed at The Starling Midtown for our anniversary, and unfortunately the experience was very disappointing
From the start, we had to wait about 10 minutes at check-in for a manager to come unlock the room, even though both we and the hotel had already confirmed the reservation
Once inside, the TV didn’t work, and even after reporting it on the day we arrived, no one ever came to fix it
The fridge also wasn’t cold at all, so the $150 worth of room service food we ordered had to be thrown away
On top of that, the billing felt misleading, and there was zero communication about parking options if we didn’t want valet
For an anniversary trip, the lack of basic amenities and the lack of any apology or offer to make things right from the hotel itself was extremely disappointing
The only reasons we gave two stars were the decor and breakfast

I recently stayed at The Starling Atlanta Midtown, Curio Collection by Hilton, and it was honestly one of the most disappointing Hilton experiences I’ve had — especially as a Diamond member who travels almost every week for work and leisure
When I checked in, I asked the front desk about a room upgrade (which I usually receive as a Diamond member)
I was told no upgrades were available because I had already chosen my room
However, when I checked the Hilton app, I saw plenty of upgraded rooms available at that exact time — very frustrating, but I didn’t argue
That night, I couldn’t sleep because the room above mine (I was on the 21st floor) had constant noise and banging until after 1 a
, then again early in the morning
I had to request a room change the next day
Later, I asked the front desk for housekeeping service to refresh the new room and change the sheets
When I came back around 11 p
, they had only cleaned the restroom — trash wasn’t emptied, no water was provided, and the sheets hadn’t been changed
To make things worse, I found hair on the bed and even my dental retainers on the floor
I reported all this to the front desk, and they told me a housekeeping manager would call me, but no one ever followed up, even though the hotel texted saying a manager would reach out
On top of that, one morning I requested my car from the valet and had to wait 35 minutes for it to show up, which caused me to be late and affected my work schedule
For a Hilton Curio Collection property, this experience was far below expectations — from the lack of professionalism to the poor housekeeping, long valet delays, and zero follow-up
Definitely not the level of service I expect from Hilton

I am a Hilton Honors member,  had a reservation for last Saturday,  and had digitally checked in- and received the digital key confirmation
All was fine, until  I arrived Saturday night  and the digital key was not there
I thought it was a simple glitch
Long story short: My reservation had been canceled by the hotel
The man at the front desk was rude, unhelpful,  and clearly either unaware of the hotel systems or, frankly and most likely, being dishonest
(He kept insisting that I must have canceled my own reservation
First,  If I did, then I wouldn't have been standing there
Second,  a little difficult to cancel a reservation while driving,  which I had been doing for 9 hours
And, most importantly,  third: Impossible- because you cannot cancel or change a reservation on the app on the day of the reservation/ after the cutoff period
He even challenged that I "must not have" checked in
UNTIL I showed him the check in digital key request from that same morning
And here's the proof that the property was not operating transparently
I told him to simply reestablish the reservation
He said they were all sold out
They had given - sold- my room away
A room that was reserved by a Hilton Honors member
A room already checked in
So unprofessional
And such a safety issue
I asked him to call the manager while I called Hilton Honors line
He did not
In the meantime,  I found another hotel in the area
Clearly,  there is a management issue- perhaps with Hilton systemically

Our stay began on a positive note; however, it unfortunately escalated into a very disappointing experience
Upon checking in and arriving on the third floor, we immediately noticed a strong odor of marijuana and cigarette smoke throughout the hallway
When we first entered our room, there was no noticeable smell, and we were grateful for that
The following day, we were away attending a work conference and spent very little time in the room
However, on the second night, the odor had become significantly more noticeable, not only in the hallway but also inside our room
Initially, we chose not to raise a complaint, as we were focused on our work obligations and assumed the issue would resolve itself
To our surprise, we were later charged a $300 smoking fee, with the explanation that “hidden smoke detectors” had been triggered in our room
This accusation is deeply concerning, as both my coworker and I are non-smokers and did not engage in any activity that would warrant such a charge
When we attempted to address the situation with staff, specifically an individual named Michael, we were met with a lack of professionalism and courtesy
Rather than working toward a resolution, we were dismissed, accused of being untruthful, and spoken to in a manner that was both inappropriate and disrespectful

In addition to the smoking fee, we were also charged an extra $220 deposit, which we were told would be refunded after checkout, as we caused no damage and left the room in the same condition it was found
Given that we spent minimal time in the room and used it solely for sleeping, these charges are especially frustrating
Overall, this experience was extremely disappointing

once
In 3 weeks
THREE!! (And by deep clean I simply mean taking a sponge to the counters and doing a vacuum and sheet change)

House keepers are notoriously overworked and underpaid - but this is the HILTON we’re talking about - not Comfort Inn or Motel 6

Please invest more time and training in your housekeepers! In our 3 week stay we had THREE different scenarios where a housekeeper walked in on us, after NO KNOCK! Not a single day (unless asked unnecessarily and repeatedly) were our toilets cleaned, our counters wiped down, our room vacuumed - in 21 days
The bathroom vents looked like they hadn’t been cleaned out in 10 years - the picture doesn’t do it justice
And below is attached pictures of how our beds were “made” many days when we’d return to the room
Absolutely shocking! I really implore the management of Hilton to take 2024 as a year of investment in your housekeeping staff and TRAINING as this is NOT the Hilton Standard
Because of my husband’s job we are limited on hotel stays and may have to stay at this property again

That was when all of the service stopped
The toilet in our room was obviously not flushing on first use (not clogged, just not flushing at all)
Tried calling guest services who transferred me to the front desk twice
No one would answer, so I had to walk down and wait in line
Two women were working the desk and would only help one person at a time (one working, one just standing there)
I got to the front of the line and told them what was going on, and there was no apology or concern shown at all
They called “engineering” and told me he’d be to my room when he could
Considering how much I paid for the room and that this hotel is supposed to be 4 star, this is unacceptable service
We can’t use our bathroom until someone shows up, and we don’t know when that will be

Editing to add: I had to go back down to the front desk after 30+ minutes and ask to be moved to a different room

Originally built in 1906 by Coca-Cola founder Asa Griggs Candler, this historic skyscraper has been meticulously restored into a boutique hotel that feels like a step back in time—but with all the 5-star modern comforts
The Architecture & Vibe
The moment you walk into the lobby, the white Georgia marble, Tiffany glass, and original brass elevators take your breath away
It’s easily one of the most Instagrammable spots in the city
Even if you aren't staying overnight, the grand staircase is worth a visit alone
Dining at "By George"
Located in what used to be the Central Bank and Trust, the hotel restaurant, By George, is a destination in its own right
• The Breakfast: The Tres Leches French Toast is legendary
• The Dinner: Don’t miss the Atlantic Sea Scallops or the Steak and Fries with cognac sauce
• The Cocktails: The bar program is top-tier
Try a classic martini—the atmosphere practically demands it
The Rooms
The rooms strike a perfect balance: high ceilings and large windows that flood the space with natural light, paired with sleek, contemporary furnishings
The bathrooms are spacious (shoutout to the luxury robes and designer toiletries) and provide a quiet sanctuary from the hustle of downtown
Pro-Tips for Your Stay:
History Buffs: Ask about the basement vault
It famously housed the secret Coca-Cola formula for years!
Location: You are steps away from the Peachtree Center MARTA station, making it easy to get to the airport or Midtown without a car
Valet: Like most downtown hotels, valet is the way to go, but be sure to call for your car 15–20 minutes early during peak hours
Bottom line: The Candler is more than just a place to sleep; it’s a piece of Atlanta history
Perfect for a romantic staycation or a sophisticated business trip

I stayed at The Candler Hotel in January 2026 as a Hilton Honors Diamond member, booking through American Express Hotel Collection
The hotel itself is stunning—the historic restoration is remarkable, the lobby popcorn is a nice touch, and there were several instances of genuinely excellent customer service during my stay
However, I experienced significant issues with benefit communication and management follow-up that required over two weeks to resolve
The Problem:
At check-in, I was explicitly told by front desk staff that my $100 Amex Hotel Collection property credit could be used for valet parking and would stack with my Diamond food & beverage benefits
I relied on this information when incurring charges
At checkout, I was informed for the first time of an undisclosed hotel policy requiring the Amex credit to be exhausted before Diamond benefits could apply
Additionally, only one of my two daily Diamond F&B credits was applied to my folio
When I raised these issues and requested correction, the front desk declined to adjust the charges, resulting in an $84 balance that would not have existed had the policies been disclosed upfront
The Bigger Issue:
I emailed the Front Office Manager the same day (as instructed by the front desk) to request review
I received no response for 17 days
I escalated to Hilton Guest Assistance twice, each time being promised a response within 48 hours
Neither promise was kept
I contacted American Express Hotel Collection, who called the hotel directly on my behalf
Even they were unable to get the charge reversed

After 17 days, five emails, two phone calls, filing BBB complaints, and initiating a credit card dispute, the General Manager finally responded and resolved the issue within 24 hours—something that should have happened on Day 1
Resolution:
To their credit, once senior management became involved, they were apologetic and provided appropriate compensation
The GM personally called to apologize, and Hilton's Executive Office followed up independently
Recommendation:
The Candler is a beautiful property with genuine potential, but there are serious training gaps around:
Accurate communication of benefit policies at check-in
Management responsiveness to guest concerns
Empowering front desk staff to correct billing errors
Until they fix these training issues, you may wish to avoid the hotel if you are a Diamond member or booking through Amex
I do hope they sort these training issues soon, as the property itself is lovely and, if possible, I would love to make this my main hotel to stay at when I am visiting Atlanta

serving beverages

My fiancé and I booked our engagement photo shoot at The Candler Hotel
We paid $1,400 for this shoot, and even paid $300 to accommodate our dog in the shoot
Grace was absolutely wonderful and professional with helping get us booked
We cannot thank her enough
The issue began with Michelle Bradley (Director of Sales)
Like most dogs, our dog began to periodically bark in the room that had been reserved for us
Michelle came over to my fiancé and I in the middle of our shoot and rudely told us that the dog had to leave because he was disturbing meetings, as she was trying to run a place of business
As a director, one has a duty to maintain the utmost professionalism at all times
Unfortunately, Michelle failed to exercise this skill
I completely understand holding a professional meeting and how a dog barking can be a distraction, however it is all about how you treat people
We paid for our dog to be included, and if an Animal barking were to be an issue, Hilton should strongly consider allowing dogs for such events, or find other accommodations for them
We love curio collection and stand by the brand but unfortunately, we will never stay at The Candler Hotel because of how we were treated
Very nice hotels with interesting history, perfect location, walking distance to Olympic Park, aquarium and the world of Coca-Cola, room is cozy and clean

A truly phenomenal boutique hotel in the heart of Atlanta

The on-site restaurant, By George, is extraordinary and easily ranks among the finest dining experiences we’ve ever had
We visited for a volleyball tournament, and the location was ideal: a short, pleasant walk to the convention center and all the main attractions
An effortless 10 out of 10
An exceptional stay — highly recommend
Amazing stay for my birthday

Overall it is a nice hotel! Great views and good location
The room was nice, clean and very roomy!
Front desk staff was cool
Very friendly and polite

Our waitress, Angie, she was great
10/10 very nice and attentive
Kudos to her
Went to the bar the next day, the bartenders were great, very friendly (wish I could remember their names for the shoutout)
Very good drinks
Breakfast was amazing
Had some minor hiccups but they were immediately addressed by Mr
Jerome in Guest Services

I'm terrible with names, but a big shout out to Kadema(?) at the front desk for making my stay so wonderful, I extended it twice!  Everyone at this hotel is focused on service
Each experience I had  from valet, bell service, front desk, the bar, and housekeeping was outstanding
My room was extremely clean and serviced every day

all met my standards

I recommend this hotel if you are staying in Downtown Atlanta
Wonderful hotel in Atlanta, the decoration is really amazing

The restaurant for breakfast and dinner was a great place and the people working there were very professional
Only disappointment was the broken rower in the fitness room (which was nice)
I hope it can be fixed soon
Extremely disappointed with our stay
We arrived and the bell staff acted like they were afraid to touch our bags, very awkward and unwelcoming
Check-in was rushed; no one explained anything besides our room number

I had called a day before, letting them know we were coming from Wisconsin for an awards banquet that started at 4pm and asked if we could check in just 30 minutes to an hour early since it was also our 12-year wedding anniversary, also emailed
No one ever replied
When I called again the next day, they said they couldn’t accommodate us
In over a decade of celebrating our anniversary at hotels, we’ve always been offered at least a room upgrade option, wine upon arrival, or a chance to pay for add-ons
This hotel offered none of that
It felt cold and corporate, not warm or luxury
The best thing about the room was the bathroom
The pillows were absolutely terrible, flat within minutes and the bed was just okay
To be honest, a stay at Comfort Suites probably would have been better
I even paid $59 for the breakfast add-on but was still charged again at checkout
Overall, this stay completely ruined what should have been a special evening
Hotel is very mid like a budget hotel
Service has been really disappointing, I honestly wish I never came here
There was no shower cap in the room, and when I called the front desk, it took forever for them to bring one up even though the hotel wasn’t busy
Such a simple request, and they couldn’t handle it efficiently
We mentioned multiple times that we had just gotten married, and not once did anyone acknowledge it during check-in or our stay
Overall, a letdown
Would not recommend coming here
Great service especially ny dining room service every night
Stayed here for 4 nights while attending a conference
First time in Atlanta and it was not a nice experience
My room was not serviced on any day I stayed here unless I called the front desk
Even after talking to front desk in person every morning before leaving for the day to ask for my room to be cleaned, I kept coming back in the evening to find my room not serviced
Management seems to have a poor communication with staff
Location is in a downtown area that is rather empty, mostly we saw unhoused people nearby
This hotel is a disappointment and I would never stay here again
The hotel has valet parking at $55/night and a single lane for arrivals on a side street
When we arrived, it took 15 minutes to be acknowledged by the one valet person working the entrance (with just one car in front of us)
No bellman present to assist with luggage
No communication from the valet person on lack of a bellman
Upon check out, I called the front desk 15 minutes early (at 5:45am) asking for my car to please be brought out
When we got downstairs to check out, no car
Another 10 minute wait for the car
I've stayed at numerous hotels with valet parking as the only option (no self-parking)
They all have a door greeter out front to communicate to guests and organize the valet and bellman persons
Other issues
The floors were dirty
The elevators looked like they were last cleaned 6 months ago
Hamptons at 1/3 of the cost are cleaner than this place
The room was super cramped
The previous reviews saying the towels are washed in vinegar and smell like pickles is spot on
I've never had towels washed in this manner at any other Hilton property
Wonderful room
Beautiful lobby
$55 for overnight parking was obscene
This Hotel Stay was a favorite and definitely will stay again if I’m back in Atlanta
The location was great
The service amazing! The ambiance was everything! It’s too many details to add honestly
If you love great service and food
(By George)
Book you a stay! You’ll love it!
My stay at this hotel was one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had
Upon check-in, everything was a smooth sail but began to defer shortly after
The front desk staff were rude, unhelpful, and completely distasteful
They showed no effort to assist with anything and made the entire check-out process unpleasant
On top of that, they withheld my incidental fee and made poor excuses about how I needed to contact my bank instead of taking responsibility or providing any real help
It felt like they just wanted to pass the blame rather than fix the issue

To make matters worse, the hotel restaurant allowed another guest to charge their meal to my room just by giving my room number — and I was billed for it! When I brought it to their attention, they shifted blame towards the restaurant staff! No apology, no ownership, and no urgency to make things right
The only kind and professional people we encountered were the room service and housekeeping staff — they were polite, respectful, and the only reason I’m even giving this hotel one star
Overall, I’m extremely disappointed
Between the unprofessional staff, billing errors, and complete lack of accountability, I would never stay here again and don’t recommend this hotel to anyone
Gorgeous architecture and amazing hospitality
The staff is great and the man whole gives the popcorn is a gentleman! Love him!!
What a very nice Hotel
When I arrived they refused to let me tote my own luggage, I enjoyed that type of service
I stayed on the 17th floor with a wonderful view of the park and got to watch the sunrise in the mornings
I really enjoyed the shower! I'm sensitive to textures but the shower floor and water pressure were quite pleasant
I could smell that the linens were washed in vinegar, I hated the smell
Imagine drying after a shower and the towels smell like pickles
Or laying in bed and smelling pickles all night
The shower door also opens oddly
Getting out the shower you have to squeeze by the toilet
Rubbing the toilet with my legs after getting out the shower me feel dirty again
The valet is $55 a night and ETREMELY SLOW and when I say extremely I mean extremely
It's a 15-20 minute wait whenever you need your car like OMG
When I walked up to the front desk once to ask about valet it was an uninviting experience
They were not rude per se, the individual just did not acknowledge me standing right in front of them at the desk
Customer service in Atlanta is
different

The bell hop informed me of some of it and told me they gave historic tours of the hotel

BUT that would explain the almost $5 per night  Historic Preservation fees I saw on the Final Bill lol
Great rooms, staff super nice and helpful

So it can get quite stuffy

I recently had the pleasure of staying at The Candler Hotel in Atlanta, to see the Braves games and I can confidently say it was a five-star experience! The hotel is perfectly situated close to everything(aquarium, world of coke, museums) making it incredibly convenient for exploring the local attractions, dining, and shopping
From the moment my husband and I arrived, the staff went above and beyond to ensure our stay was enjoyable
They were friendly, attentive, and always ready to assist with any questions or requests
It’s evident that they genuinely care about their guests’ experiences
The hotel itself is beautifully designed with a blend of historic charm and modern amenities including the elegant onsite restaurant
Though only eating breakfast here, we were quickly served, food was exquisite and experienced 5 star service!
Our room was spacious, comfortable, and impeccably clean, providing a perfect retreat after a day of sightseeing
Overall, I highly recommend The Candler Hotel for anyone visiting Atlanta
The combination of its prime location, amazing staff, and delightful accommodations made my stay truly unforgettable!
The hotel has a good reputation and a wow factor with its entrance area and staircase, but unfortunately that's about it
The team does its job, but that's all
The location is good due to the situation with homeless people in Atlanta, but we didn't feel safe (of course, the hotel can't be blamed for that)
The rooms are okay, but considering the price, they are very uninviting and don't match the hotel's image
Currently staying here but not too pleased
The tap and water fountain water have a terrible moldy taste, there was no bottled water in the room
The prices of bottled water were $8 to $15
When I inqured about other water sources (restaurant?) they then agreed to give us two hotel brand water bottles and would not give us any extra despite none being in the room initially
The hours on the Google page are also incorrect for By George which should have been updated
They do not open at 4pm for dinner, the correct time is 5:30pm
Will update review if stay improves through other means
Exceptional! Elegant! Staff is top notch! From vallet to check in, all the way to helping you with your items to your room! If you're ever in Atlanta, you must stay at the Candler!
Beautiful hotel
Restaurant has great food and service
Hotel staff is wonderful
We were misled about the $30 a day credit
Initially when we asked on the first day, we were told we didn’t qualify for it
Then on the second day, another person stated we did, and that we could use the entire amount in one transaction
Based on this, we spent $90 at the restaurant on the second day
When we checked out, we were informed that we could only use $30 of the credit each day
Even though the person on the first day gave us completely incorrect information that caused us to lose out on the initial $30, the hotel would do nothing to correct this mistake
Terrible and misleading service for a hotel that’s supposed to be high end
Booked a room for The Candler Hotel last month for a concert
I was really excited as the pictures looked beautiful
Unfortunately, life had other plans and I ended up staying in the hospital with my grandmother who fell and broke her hip
I called the hotel at 7 to cancel my stay and after a 10 minute wait was informed that, because I am a team member, I was unable to cancel and was charged a $75 no show fee
Nonrefundable
Crazy
My nana is doing okay thankfully which is all that matters but the front desk employee was also very curt with me and honestly very unhelpful
Really really disappointed in the service I received
Best stay I've had in Atlanta!
I've been vacationing in Atlanta for 4 summers in a row now and my stay at the Candler was by far the best
Location is great and it was easy to walk to everything I wanted to do
The rooms were SO CLEAN
The hotel itself is beautiful and kept in incredible condition
Food is excellent whether you get room service or go down to the restaurant
Staff are the friendliest and most professional in the city
My husband and I absolutely loved staying here and we definitely plan on coming again!
The room was clean, the service was very professional checking in and out were a breze!
I recently had the pleasure of staying at this hotel and wanted to share my experience
While the hotel has an old feel to it, I found it to be very clean and well-maintained

I would especially like to commend Natalia and John Paul

Positive: A room listed as wheelchair accessible really was wheelchair accessible Negative: Streets and sidewalk in front of hotel were blocked off even a nearby road was com
, pletely   blocked  off  due to another hotels construction
Recently stayed at The Candler for a night and it was so nice

The staff was very nice and check in and out was a breeze
Will definitely stay at this hotel again 10/10

The hosts at the restaurant were also not kind or welcoming, and we were refused a table when we tried going down for appetizers and drinks
The restaurant only had a couple of tables seated at the time, and the bar was full, so we ended up going elsewhere
The only good service we received was from some of the room service staff
Just a very frustrating experience and I wouldn’t go back
Location is great
Hotel is beautiful
I’m rating mainly based on communication and policies that are unclear
They say pet friendly, but do not specify that this is dog specific ANYWHERE
You have to call them and double check
I’m glad I did as I planned to have my cat with me, but was told over the phone they do not allow cats
Please make this obvious somewhere on the website when booking
I was also told on another phone call the day before I arrived that early check in before noon was a $25 fee
I then planned to check in after noon
When I came to check in, the person at the front desk told me “oh no, the fee is before 1pm” and charged me the fee
I didn’t want to deal with the hassle of coming back in an hour so I paid and tried to explain the situation
He told me the policy “changes” sometimes… so odd
It is very frustrating being told one thing only to find out it’s another
Prior to the start of my stay, the Hilton app offered a potential $200 late check out option
When I called the front desk the day of check out - they said the fee was actually $75 for a 2pm check out
I just feel like this fee schedule should be the same across the board?
Additionally, I don’t think the location is super accessible for people with disabilities or a lot of luggage
They have a side entrance with a very loud and slow service elevator that you have to flag someone down to use since they have to unlock it
I understand it’s an older building, but that entrance also has a ramp that leads to a small set of stairs to get to the lobby
I’m not sure why they didn’t just make it a full ramp?
With that being said, the staff was regularly near the doors and stairs to help you with your luggage and they were very kind and helpful
Stayed for Dragoncon and loved it! We stayed in a king suite and the tub was divine after a long day
The hotel is beautiful and clean, staff are super friendly and the restaurant was delicious!
Gorgeous historical hotel
Comfortable beds

Super friendly staff!
We stayed here for the night while attending Dragoncon
Our experience at every level, from the service to the food, was incredible
We booked a double queen room, but got upgraded to a very large suite on the 14th floor
The panoramic views were fantastic
The huge bathroom had a tub large enough to accommodate me at 6'4"; Which I took full advantage of after a day of walking around
The hotel itself is beautiful
They've clearly done a lot of work to maintain the original look of this stunning old building
Overall, we had an excellent experience
I'd highly recommend staying here
If you do, order some of the Cornbread Muffins from the Hotels restaurant
I dont usually like Cornbread but I could eat those muffins every day
Seriously, they were unbelievably delicious!
I recently stayed at The Candler Hotel Atlanta, Curio Collection by Hilton, and it was outstanding from start to finish
The front desk team, especially Joy and her supervisor, made check in and every interaction feel welcoming and genuinely attentive
JP at the bar brought fantastic energy to the evenings with engaging, knowledgeable service, and the food and the valet staff were efficient and responsive to every need
The rooms were incredibly stylish, spacious, and impeccably clean, with thoughtful touches throughout

ah dang

We rocked “The Staircase” (a/k/a Legal Crime % Psy | Atty Conspiracy Thriller) via HBOMax and Time Warner

| Meanwhile back in Forsyth County… So far Todd (also a wedding Crashers reference… Everytime 😂) I do believe The Gowans Firm has the best rendering of the Courthouse for promotional use and we may need a touch up and gifted incentives to help us all w the next steps
“We’ll be back” 🤷‍♀️🔔 Film Foco already
The rooms were clean and comfortable and quiet and stylish

Im giving 5 stars on service for the restaurant because everyone we encountered there was so pleasant
Brian the manager, Judith, Brian, and Irish the servers, and Dayje (sp?) at the front were lovely and welcoming
The foid was also good--though more vegetarian options are always welcome
The tea and coffee are bottomless! Yay!  The building is GORGEOUS
Its worth wandering around it just to see the architechture
Decent hotel located conveniently in downtown Atlanta
I give a 2-star rating because of the lack of self-parking options, as well as the cost of the valet
Having to use valet was a huge inconvenience and caused us to have to do additional planning rather than having a parking garage accessible at any time by guests

No dedicated loading zone or unloading zone- had to leave key with valet then call car around again just to set our belongings in our room, use the restroom after a 6 hour drive, then turn around and leave
There was also no security and homeless people went in and out of the hotel on multiple occasions, while valet staff had to act as security on one occasion
They need to have some sort of keycard access and a 24 hour monitored door to get in and out of the hotel so that homeless people are not able to walk in and use amenities
Hotel also did not have a microwave in rooms
Elevators were also shaky, made a lot of noise, and seemed like they needed maintenance
Besides all of these things, the room itself was pleasant and decently quiet due to the floor that we picked not having much guest traffic on it
Probably would not stay here again
Dwight is the man, extremely accommodating
Excellent decor and ambiance
The room was large, the bathroom was very nice
The bed was comfortable
Beautiful hotel, wonderful food and amazing customer service!
This is a unique classy hotel
We enjoyed our stay
The staff was helpful
In fact, Natalya was very resourceful and creative to help us find some last minute takeout boxes to use for plates for our pizza
It is a close walk to the aquarium
Came here to celebrate my husband’s Birthday & they did nothing but Exceed Our Expectations!
Natalia assisted me with special arrangements & Mr
Jerome went ABOVE & BEYOND MAKING Our Stay Extremely Special
He was so personable & hospitable during our stay
It’s the little touches that make memories like this so memorable
Breakfast was quick & delicious !
JJ at the front desk was so sweet also
The suite was Spacious, Clean & was equipped  with everything we needed
Old ornate building needs some upgrades
Floor boards made noises when walking around room
Bathroom was quite spacious for King room
Location depressing, surrounded by lots of homeless and desolate downtown area with no shops, restaurants
The Underground nearby was a ghost town every evening
You will never be able to tell that the Candler Hotel was once a bank and was the tallest building in the whole of Atlanta
The bank vault housed the original recipe for Coca-Cola which you can visit
Dwayne was fantastic in meticulously explaining everything about this historic building
Including that the sun would come in and hit the chandelier
The reflection would then hit the tiles and that’s how the tiles got their colour!
The building became a hotel in 2019 which now makes it a grand 119 years old! It has been meticulously kept and very much loved
The Art Deco theme that runs throughout is exemplary

Its grandeur in the main foyer becomes apparent when you feast your eyes upon the staircase that dominates the hall with its sweeping curves drawing the eye upwards towards the majestic chandelier (made of Tiffany glass and original from 1906)
The reception area is warm and inviting
Their complimentary Punch should come with a warning as it is delicious and highly addictive! Not to be bypassed
All of the staff are wonderful and professional
A special mention for Jerome (Bell Captain of Candler) a wonderful gentleman who made my day with his smile and conversation
Our room (#311) carries out the Art Deco theme
Once again it was immaculate
The huge windows let the light flood in and showed us the hustle and bustle of a city that doesn’t sleep
It’s also the first hotel ever (that we have stayed at) to have a black makeup towel! Now that’s a genius idea
There was only an Espresso maker in our room so we called the front desk to ask if there was a kettle as we like our tea very hot
We were informed that the Espresso maker can also be used for hot water (which doesn’t really heat the water for our British cup of tea!)
That was the end of the conversation for us
However when we returned after a long day out much to our surprise there was a kettle waiting for us! What a lovely surprise
Thank you
Would be stay here again? Absolutely! Without a shadow of a doubt…Yes Yes and Yes
This is the ideal location as across the street is the Peachtree Center Marta Station if you like exploring
Me and my friend stopped by around 5:45pm today, because it looked beautiful and we were considering it for future stays, just wanting to glance around the public areas

( he was middle age man just standing at the entrance with security guy) was incredibly rude and aggressive right away, telling us we couldn't go upstairs or take any pictures
When we tried to explain we were just looking, he called security to remove us

Be warned if you're thinking of just dropping by to check it out!
But security guy was cool and understood us :)

This hotel is lovely, a special shout out to the amazing staff who really went above and beyond to make our stay special, this place is is powered by outstanding staff , and we love you guys, thanks for looking after us 🥰❤️
Spent a night here with my family
The beds were v comfortable, and the bathroom was large n clean
We all slept v well
We didn't have breakfast there due to its a la carte option
We prefer buffet breakfast options
We were checked in by a friendly n professional member of staff called JJ
We just had a wonderful weekend in downtown Atlanta staying at The Candler
The hotel is exquisite and the chef at their restaurant, By George is superb
We especially enjoyed our server, Shakay for brunch both Saturday and Sunday
She is wonderful and made our experience even better
My Fiancé and I thoroughly enjoyed our stay at The Candler Hotel
The staff is friendly and accommodated our request to check in early
The onsite restaurant had the most delicious and flaky homemade corn bread! Our waitress, Shakay, offered great service and welcomed our customized orders
After this visit, we are already looking forward to coming back!
Liked the hotel
The valet staff were really kind and accommodating
The room was nice and clean
The first night we had some issues with the lights
For some reason we couldn’t get them to turn out but they started working eventually
We booked this hotel for its proximity to Mercedes Benz
It’s a good 25 minute walk in comfy shoes
Overall I’d stay here again
I was really looking forward to celebrating my girlfriend’s birthday at The Candler Hotel, but unfortunately, our experience was disappointing
As we were heading out to dinner, my girlfriend wanted to take a quick birthday photo in the lobby—a simple and special moment
However, we were abruptly interrupted by a man who appeared to be security (or hotel staff), and his behavior was extremely disrespectful and rude
Instead of politely addressing us, he approached with unnecessary hostility, completely ruining the moment for her
This was supposed to be a joyful evening, and it was honestly embarrassing and upsetting
For a hotel that presents itself as upscale, the lack of courtesy and professionalism from staff is unacceptable
I hope management takes this seriously, because no guest—especially one celebrating a birthday—should be made to feel disrespected or unwelcome
Nice location
Not super fancy, but upscale for a fun weekend
Building has character
The staff was very helpful
Especially when we checked out
We left at 3am and they brought the car around quickly and loaded up our car
We drove so we had a lot of stuff with us
Will definitely stay here again
I am beyond impressed by the service at this hotel
I had someone instantly get my stuff for me and stick with me through the whole check in and bring my stuff up to my room
Jermaine (hopefully I spelled that right) was simply amazing and made this experience 10x better
I stayed 7/12 - 7/15 and this was the best hotel I have ever EVER stayed at
If you ever need to be in Downtown Atlanta for whatever reason, please stay at the Candler Hotel
Jermaine, one of the employees, was so gracious and welcoming to me
He made me feel so comfortable and helped me with my luggage with no questions asked!
Loved my recent stay here! The staff was super helpful and kind
Jermaine was always eager to provide suggestions and carry bags

Other than that, I had a perfect stay!

We came for the Cowboy Carter concert and had an excellent experience! The valet staff were incredibly kind and helpful, not just with the car and bags but with knowledge of the area, recommendations, and directions
Jermaine was a pleasure to speak to during the short wait for our car and made loading up super smooth and enjoyable
The staff absolutely made our stay and made us happy and excited to be in Atlanta!! 100/10 recommend
From the moment we were greeted by Jermaine at the front, we were thrilled with the service, friendliness of staff, and quality of the gorgeous hotel
Comfortable and stylish rooms, convenient location for walking around downtown and close to Marta stations, we would definitely stay here again!!
In Atlanta for a couple of days and spent my first night at this beautiful and historic hotel

I was equally pleased when I entered my room, it was like I had my own luxury apartment with a great view of downtown Atlanta

I don’t normally rave about the bathroom in a hotel room but this bathroom is truly luxurious! The bed was extremely comfortable and the air conditioning worked great, keeping the room nice and cool, very important to me
It was also extremely quiet
Highly recommend this lovely hotel for your next visit to Atlanta!
This place was awesome
From the second we pulled up Jermaine was greeting us

But other than that… it was a really friendly and clean! Next trip to Atlanta, I will stay here again
We stayed for a weekend at the Candler and it was an amazing experience!

Natalia checked in a couple of times to make sure we had everything we needed

Thank you, Jermaine!

My family and I recently had two stays at the Hilton Garden Inn Chattanooga/Hamilton Place, chosen for its convenient location as a halfway point on our travels
While the hotel offered some expected amenities, several aspects of our experience left much to be desired, particularly regarding customer service
Front Desk Experience (1/5 Stars):
Unfortunately, the most consistent and disappointing aspect of both stays was the demeanor of the man working the front desk
On both occasions, his attitude was incredibly uninviting, bordering on rude
During our first stay, when we requested an extra towel (as only three were provided for four guests), I was told to pick it up myself
After a 15-minute wait at the front desk with no one present, my second attempt to retrieve the towel was met with an unhelpful and surly response

For our second visit, I proactively requested an extra towel and two extra pillows at check-in, only to be told that extra pillows might not be available – a surprising and frustrating response from a hotel
Amenities & Breakfast (2/5 Stars):
The pool was closed during our first stay, which was an inconvenience for a family traveling with children, though not a deal-breaker
It was open during our second visit
The breakfast, which incurs an additional charge, was described as "okay
" However, no information or directions regarding breakfast were provided at check-in on either occasion, requiring us to seek out details ourselves
Room Condition (3/5 Stars):
We ended up in the exact same room for both stays
The room was generally fairly clean
However, there was a noticeable and unpleasant stain on one of the chairs
Given our previous interactions, we opted not to address it with the front desk and simply avoided using the chair
Customer Service vs
Hotel Promise (1/5 Stars):
The most ironic part of our stay was the presence of posters throughout the hotel assuring guests that staff were "happy to help" with anything needed to make their stay more enjoyable
This statement felt entirely disconnected from our actual experience, where requests were met with reluctance or a poor attitude
Conclusion:

In summary, if you are looking for an average hotel with a fairly clean room, an "okay" breakfast you pay extra for, and are prepared to be treated like an inconvenience throughout your stay, the Hilton Garden Inn Chattanooga/Hamilton Place might be for you
Given the availability of other similarly priced or cheaper hotels in the area, this Hilton Garden Inn will unfortunately not be my first, second, or even third choice for future stays

Extremely disappointing experience from Hilton London Metropole
This property markets itself as a five star hotel, but in execution, it functions as a solid four star at best
Given the premium room rates of $500 to $700 per night, the actual experience fell incredibly short of expectations
As a Diamond member, the arrival experience was disappointing
There was a lengthy wait at reception with no dedicated lane for top tier loyalty members
The front desk staff appeared aloof, exhausted, and entirely disengaged, providing a remarkably cold and uninviting welcome
Furthermore, there was no welcome note or standard recognition of status upon entering the room
The room itself was acceptable, offering a minor upgrade to a high floor with a balcony, but preparation was entirely overlooked
Despite spending three hours coordinating via the Hilton app chat prior to arrival, none of my basic requests for extra pillows, water, or coffee were fulfilled
The communication on the app was slow, unhelpful, and ultimately resulted in empty promises
Having to repeatedly request these basic items after check in turned the stay into unnecessary work
The Executive Lounge was functional but highly underwhelming, suffering from overcrowding and a very basic selection of food and beverages
Ironically, I have experienced far more sincere, gracious hospitality at three star properties over the past year
While the stay ended without further incident, the overall lack of warmth and poor execution means I will not be returning
For travelers looking for a genuine, true luxury experience in London, I strongly advise considering actual five star hotels nearby, such as The Wellesley Knightsbridge
The contrast in staff professionalism, room quality, and overall execution makes it clear that this Hilton property simply does not belong in the same luxury category
