Extremely disappointing experience associated with the London Hilton on Park Lane on St George’s Day
My party and I had spent the afternoon enjoying a formal luncheon nearby before deciding to visit Shanghai Me within the hotel for drinks
We were all smartly dressed in suits, wearing St George’s member's ties and red roses in respectful celebration of the national day
Despite being appropriately dressed and behaving respectfully, we were refused entry
The situation left us feeling unfairly judged purely for wearing tasteful national symbols associated with St George’s Day
What made matters worse was the handling of the complaint afterwards
Different explanations were given, including claims that we had been offered the opportunity to remove items of clothing, which simply did not happen
The final response ultimately relied on “management discretion” while avoiding any meaningful acknowledgement of how the experience came across from a customer perspective
I fully appreciate that private venues have dress codes and reserve the right to refuse entry
However, there is a significant difference between enforcing standards and making guests feel unwelcome because of respectful expressions of national identity
Most disappointing of all was Hilton corporate distancing itself from the matter entirely despite the venue operating within one of its flagship London hotels
For a hotel and venue positioning themselves as luxury hospitality brands, I expected far better communication, professionalism, and customer care
🏨 What it is (simple definition)
A classic 5-star luxury hotel run by Hilton, sitting on Park Lane in Mayfair, overlooking Hyde Park—one of the most prestigious hotel addresses in London
📍 Location (major advantage)
Right on Park Lane, Mayfair
Facing Hyde Park
Walking distance to:
Buckingham Palace
Green Park
Oxford Street shopping
Extremely central for sightseeing and business travel
🏛️ Scale & style
Opened in 1963 as Hilton’s first UK hotel
One of London’s early modern skyscraper hotels
~28 floors, tall city views over London skyline
Style: modern corporate luxury + classic Hilton comfort, not boutique or ultra-trendy
🛏️ Rooms & experience
Around 453 rooms + 56 suites
High floors = best views (Hyde Park / skyline)
Rooms are:
Comfortable and spacious for central London
Recently refreshed in many areas
Suites often include access to Executive Lounge (breakfast + drinks)
🍽️ Dining & bars
Multiple restaurants and bars inside
Popular highlights:
Park Corner Brasserie (modern British dining)
Revery Bar (cocktails & lounge vibe)
Strong focus on convenience rather than Michelin-style exclusivity
✨ What makes it special
🔥 One of London’s most famous Hilton flagship properties
🌳 Direct Hyde Park views (rare in central London hotels)
🏙️ High-rise skyline views (great at night)
🚶 Perfect base for first-time London visitors
👍 Why people choose it
Excellent central location
Reliable international 5-star service
Good Hilton Honors benefits (upgrades/lounges)
Easy access to major landmarks
⚠️ Things to know
Not a boutique or “ultra-luxury storytelling” hotel
Can feel busy / corporate
Luxury level is solid but not on the level of ultra-premium brands nearby
🧠 Simple summary
London Hilton on Park Lane = iconic high-rise luxury hotel in Mayfair with unbeatable location, Hyde Park views, and dependable 5-star Hilton comfort

My recent stay at London Hilton on Park Lane was, overall, a thoroughly enjoyable experience, deserving of a solid four-star commendation
From the outset, the hotel impresses with its prime position overlooking Hyde Park, offering some of the most captivating views in London
There is an immediate sense of scale and energy upon arrival, with a lobby that is both lively and well-appointed, striking a balance between modern comfort and classic city hotel charm
The room itself was well presented, clean, and thoughtfully arranged
While not overly extravagant, it delivered everything one would expect from a reputable establishment of this calibre
The true highlight, however, was undoubtedly the view — waking up to the London skyline added a certain refinement to the stay that elevated the overall experience
Service throughout was professional and efficient
Staff were courteous and accommodating, particularly during check-in and within the communal areas
While there were moments where the pace felt slightly brisk — perhaps a reflection of the hotel’s popularity — it never detracted significantly from the overall comfort of the stay
Facilities were equally commendable, with the executive spaces and dining options offering a pleasant environment to unwind
The atmosphere carries a certain cosmopolitan vibrancy, making it well suited for both business and leisure travellers
If one were to offer a minor critique, it would be that certain aspects of the property feel a touch dated when compared to some of London’s newer luxury offerings
However, this is more than offset by its location, views, and consistently reliable service
In summary, the London Hilton on Park Lane delivers a polished and dependable experience in one of the city’s most desirable locations
It may not seek to overwhelm with extravagance, but it succeeds in providing comfort, convenience, and a quietly confident standard of hospitality

Does not reflect the Hilton name and is absolutely not worth the price
My stay from 31 December to 3 January was unfortunately not a very pleasant one
I had hoped to enjoy a nice New Year’s break at the hotel
The property has a long history and, although it has been renovated, there were several comfort issues
The central air conditioning was extremely noisy, and the windows did not insulate well against the cold or wind
The room felt slightly warmer only when the curtains were fully closed
A portable heater was provided, but the room was still cold unless the noisy air conditioning was left on
There also appeared to be an air deodorising device in the room, which produced a high-pitched mechanical sound and emitted a strong disinfectant-like smell that was quite unpleasant
In addition, possibly due to ageing plumbing, there was occasionally an unpleasant odour from the bathroom if the toilet lid was left open
The room was on a low floor
Although described as facing Hyde Park, the actual view was mainly rooftops of nearby buildings
The room was positioned in a narrow gap in this buildings, meaning there was very little privacy — with the curtains open and lights on, it was easy to see into neighbouring rooms and vice versa
Unfortunately, I also left behind a new limited-edition white Hello Kitty hair band which recently purchased during another trip at check-out
I contacted the hotel within a few hours, but it could not be located despite several follow-ups
As it was white and left on the bed, it may have been overlooked during housekeeping
None of these issues were the fault of the hotel staff
The hotel was extremely busy over the New Year period, and there were no alternative rooms available
Hilton, of course, cannot be compared with five-star or ultra-luxury brands, and perhaps my expectations were too high to begin with
That said, you do get what you pay for
The pricing is relatively reasonable for the area, and the location is excellent
From a value-for-money perspective, it is still a workable option if budget and location are the main priorities

This was my second stay at the Hilton Atlanta Airport, and unfortunately it confirmed that the shuttle and guest service issues I experienced before were not isolated incidents
During my first stay, the airport shuttle was over an hour late
While inconvenient, the rest of the stay was fine, so I gave the hotel the benefit of the doubt and assumed it was a one-time problem
On my most recent stay, the same issue occurred—only worse
I arrived at the airport at 8:40 PM and was at the hotel shuttle pickup area by 8:57 PM
According to the hotel’s information and app, the shuttle runs at :15 and :45
I remained at the pickup location the entire time along with several other guests, yet no shuttle arrived at 9:15, 9:45, or 10:15 PM
The shuttle finally arrived at 10:21 PM, resulting in nearly a 1
5-hour wait
During this time, there was no way to reach the hotel, and no updates or communication were provided
After check-in, I asked to speak with a manager about the situation
I was told no manager was on duty and that a supervisor would address my concern
I calmly and respectfully explained the situation and timeline
The supervisor repeatedly asked how I eventually arrived at the hotel, which gave the impression that because I did arrive, the shuttle was only a few minutes late
This line of questioning completely disregarded the fact that three scheduled shuttle times were missed
Rather than acknowledging the delay, she became defensive, implied that we somehow missed all the shuttle runs, and dismissed the situation as “no big deal
” When I continued to calmly restate the facts, she said she “couldn’t talk to me like this,” which felt disrespectful and unprofessional
I rarely leave negative reviews and have been a loyal Hilton guest for many years, but this was one of the poorest guest service experiences I’ve had with the Hilton brand
An airport hotel should provide reliable transportation and professional, respectful customer service
I would not recommend this property to travelers base on my encounter

” I found this response unnecessarily harsh and disrespectful
